 /// Channel error\n98\t    #[error(\"Channel error: {0}\")]\n99\t    ChannelError(String),\n100\t}\n101\t\n102\t/// Events emitted by the log watcher.\n103\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n104\tpub enum LogWatcherEvent {\n105\t    /// An API call was parsed from the logs\n106\t    ApiCallParsed { call: ApiCall },\n107\t\n108\t    /// A new log file was discovered\n109\t    FileDiscovered { path: PathBuf, worker_id: String },\n110\t\n111\t    /// A log file was rotated\n112\t    FileRotated { path: PathBuf, worker_id: String },\n113\t\n114\t    /// An error occurred\n115\t    Error { message: String },\n116\t}\n117\t\n118\t/// Configuration for the log watcher.\n119\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n120\tpub struct LogWatcherConfig {\n121\t    /// Directory to watch for log files\n122\t    pub log_dir: PathBuf,\n123\t\n124\t    /// Debounce duration for file system events\n125\t    pub debounce_duration: Duration,\n126\t\n127\t    /// Poll interval for reading new content\n128\t    pub poll_interval: Duration,\n129\t\n130\t    /// Channel buffer size\n131\t    pub channel_buffer: usize,\n132\t\n133\t    /// Whether to parse existing log files on startup\n134\t    pub parse_existing: bool,\n135\t}\n136\t\n137\timpl Default for LogWatcherConfig {\n138\t    fn default() -> Self {\n139\t        let home = std::env::var(\"HOME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"/tmp\".to_string());\n140\t        Self {\n141\t            log_dir: PathBuf::from(home).join(DEFAULT_LOG_DIR),\n142\t            debounce_duration: Duration::from_millis(DEFAULT_DEBOUNCE_MS),\n143\t            poll_interval: Duration::from_millis(DEFAULT_POLL_INTERVAL_MS),\n144\t            channel_buffer: DEFAULT_CHANNEL_BUFFER,\n145\t            parse_existing: false,\n146\t        }\n147\t    }\n148\t}\n149\t\n150\timpl LogWatcherConfig {\n151\t    /// Create a new config with the given log directory.\n152\t    pub fn new(log_dir: PathBuf) -> Self {\n153\t        Self {\n154\t            log_dir,\n155\t            ..Default::default()\n156\t        }\n157\t    }\n158\t\n159\t    /// Set the debounce duration.\n160\t    pub fn with_debounce(mut self, duration: Duration) -> Self {\n161\t        self.debounce_duration = duration;\n162\t        self\n163\t    }\n164\t\n165\t    /// Set the poll interval.\n166\t    pub fn with_poll_interval(mut self, interval: Duration) -> Self {\n167\t        self.poll_interval = interval;\n168\t        self\n169\t    }\n170\t\n171\t    /// Set whether to parse existing files on startup.\n172\t    pub fn with_parse_existing(mut self, parse: bool) -> Self {\n173\t        self.parse_existing = parse;\n174\t        self\n175\t    }\n176\t}\n177\t\n178\t/// Parse task-related events from log lines for performance tracking.\n179\t///\n180\t/// This function extracts task lifecycle events (start, complete, fail)\n181\t/// from worker log entries to enable performance metrics tracking.\n182\tpub fn parse_task_event(line: &str, worker_id: &str) -> Option<TaskEvent> {\n183\t    // Skip non-JSON lines\n184\t    if !line.starts_with('{') {\n185\t        return None;\n186\t    }\n187\t\n188\t    let value: Value = match serde_json::from_str(line) {\n189\t        Ok(v) => v,\n190\t        Err(_) => return None,\n191\t    };\n192\t\n193\t    // Check event type\n194\t    let event_type = value.get(\"type\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or(\"\");\n195\t\n196\t    match event_type {\n197\t        // Task start event - look for tool_use or task initiation\n198\t        \"tool_use\" | \"task_start\" => {\n199\t            let task_id = value.get(\"bead_id\")\n200\t                .or_else(|| value.get(\"task_id\"))\n201\t                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n202\t                .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n203\t                .to_string();\n204\t\n205\t            let model = value.get(\"model\")\n206\t                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n207\t                .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n208\t                .to_string();\n209\t\n210\t            Some(TaskEvent::Started {\n211\t                task_id,\n212\t                worker_id: worker_id.to_string(),\n213\t                model,\n214\t            })\n215\t        }\n216\t\n217\t        // Task completion event - result type with success/failure\n218\t        \"result\" => {\n219\t            let task_id = value.get(\"bead_id\")\n220\t                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n221\t                .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n222\t                .to_string();\n223\t\n224\t            // Check for success/failure\n225\t            let subtype = value.get(\"subtype\")\n226\t                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n227\t                .unwrap_or(\"\");\n228\t\n229\t            let success = subtype == \"success\";\n230\t\n231\t            // Extract duration if available\n232\t            let duration_ms = value.get(\"duration_ms\")\n233\t                .or_else(|| value.get(\"duration_api_ms\"))\n234\t                .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n235\t                .unwrap_or(0);\n236\t\n237\t            // Extract usage data\n238\t            let usage = value.get(\"usage\");\n239\t            let (input_tokens, output_tokens) = if let Some(u) = usage {\n240\t                let input = u.get(\"input_tokens\").and_then(|v| v.as_i64()).unwrap_or(0);\n241\t                let output = u.get(\"output_tokens\").and_then(|v| v.as_i64()).unwrap_or(0);\n242\t                (input, output)\n243\t            } else {\n244\t                (0, 0)\n245\t            };\n246\t\n247\t            // Extract or calculate cost\n248\t            let cost_usd = value.get(\"total_cost_usd\")\n249\t                .and_then(|v| v.as_f64())\n250\t                .or_else(|| {\n251\t                    // Try to calculate from usage if pricing available\n252\t                    if input_tokens > 0 || output_tokens > 0 {\n253\t                        // Rough estimate: Opus pricing\n254\t                        let cost = (input_tokens as f64 * 15.0 / 1_000_000.0)\n255\t                            + (output_tokens as f64 * 75.0 / 1_000_000.0);\n256\t                        Some(cost)\n257\t                    } else {\n258\t                        None\n259\t                    }\n260\t                })\n261\t                .unwrap_or(0.0);\n262\t\n263\t            if success {\n264\t                Some(TaskEvent::Completed {\n265\t                    task_id,\n266\t                    worker_id: worker_id.to_string(),\n267\t                    success: true,\n268\t                    duration_ms,\n269\t                    input_tokens,\n270\t                    output_tokens,\n271\t                    cost_usd,\n272\t                })\n273\t            } else {\n274\t                Some(TaskEvent::Failed {\n275\t                    task_id,\n276\t                    worker_id: worker_id.to_string(),\n277\t                    error: subtype.to_string(),\n278\t                })\n279\t            }\n280\t        }\n281\t\n282\t        _ => None,\n283\t    }\n284\t}\n285\t\n286\t/// Tracked file state for incremental parsing.\n287\t#[derive(Debug)]\n288\tstruct TrackedFile {\n289\t    /// Current file position (bytes read so far)\n290\t    position: u64,\n291\t\n292\t    /// File inode for rotation detection (Unix only)\n293\t    #[cfg(unix)]\n294\t    inode: Option<u64>,\n295\t\n296\t    /// Worker ID extracted from filename\n297\t    worker_id: String,\n298\t}\n299\t\n300\t/// Real-time log watcher that monitors log files and parses API usage metrics.\n301\tpub struct LogWatcher {\n302\t    /// Log directory being watched\n303\t    log_dir: PathBuf,\n304\t\n305\t    /// Log parser for extracting API calls\n306\t    parser: LogParser,\n307\t\n308\t    /// Tracked files with their positions\n309\t    tracked_files: HashMap<PathBuf, TrackedFile>,\n310\t\n311\t    /// The debounced file watcher (stored for proper cleanup)\n312\t    _debouncer: Option<DebouncerType>,\n313\t}\n314\t\n315\timpl LogWatcher {\n316\t    /// Create a new log watcher with default configuration.\n317\t    pub fn new(config: LogWatcherConfig) -> Result<(Self, mpsc::Receiver<LogWatcherEvent>), LogWatcherError> {\n318\t        // Create the log directory if it doesn't exist\n319\t        if !config.log_dir.exists() {\n320\t            std::fs::create_dir_all(&config.log_dir).map_err(|e| {\n321\t                LogWatcherError::InitError(format!(\n322\t                    \"Failed to create log directory {:?}: {}\",\n323\t                    config.log_dir, e\n324\t                ))\n325\t            })?;\n326\t        }\n327\t\n328\t        let (event_tx, event_rx) = mpsc::channel();\n329\t\n330\t        // Start file system watcher\n331\t        // In tests: use PollWatcher to avoid inotify instance limits\n332\t        // In production: use RecommendedWatcher (inotify on Linux) for efficiency\n333\t        let log_dir_clone = config.log_dir.clone();\n334\t        let event_tx_clone = event_tx.clone();\n335\t\n336\t        #[cfg(test)]\n337\t        let mut debouncer = {\n338\t            // Use PollWatcher in tests to avoid inotify instance limits\n339\t            // Configure short poll interval and content comparison for reliable test behavior\n340\t            let poll_interval = config.poll_interval;\n341\t            new_debouncer_opt::<_, PollWatcher, _>(\n342\t                config.debounce_duration,\n343\t                None,\n344\t                move |result: DebounceEventResult| {\n345\t                    match result {\n346\t                        Ok(events) => {\n347\t                            for event in events {\n348\t                                if let Err(e) = process_file_event(&event.event, &log_dir_clone, &event_tx_clone) {\n349\t                                    let _ = event_tx_clone.send(LogWatcherEvent::Error {\n350\t                                        message: e.to_string(),\n351\t                                    });\n352\t                                }\n353\t                            }\n354\t                        }\n355\t                        Err(errors) => {\n356\t                            for error in errors {\n357\t                                warn!(\"File watcher error: {:?}\", error);\n358\t                            }\n359\t                        }\n360\t                    }\n361\t                },\n362\t                NoCache::new(),\n363\t                notify::Config::default()\n364\t                    .with_poll_interval(poll_interval)\n365\t                    .with_compare_contents(true),\n366\t            )\n367\t            .map_err(|e| LogWatcherError::InitError(format!(\"Failed to create debouncer: {}\", e)))?\n368\t        };\n369\t\n370\t        #[cfg(not(test))]\n371\t        let mut debouncer = {\n372\t            new_debouncer_opt::<_, RecommendedWatcher, _>(\n373\t                config.debounce_duration,\n374\t                None,\n375\t                move |result: DebounceEventResult| {\n376\t                    match result {\n377\t                        Ok(events) => {\n378\t                            for event in events {\n379\t                                if let Err(e) = process_file_event(&event.event, &log_dir_clone, &event_tx_clone) {\n380\t                                    let _ = event_tx_clone.send(LogWatcherEvent::Error {\n381\t                                        message: e.to_string(),\n382\t                                    });\n383\t                                }\n384\t                            }\n385\t                        }\n386\t                        Err(errors) => {\n387\t                            for error in errors {\n388\t                                warn!(\"File watcher error: {:?}\", error);\n389\t                            }\n390\t                        }\n391\t                    }\n392\t                },\n393\t                RecommendedCache::new(),\n394\t                notify::Config::default(),\n395\t            )\n396\t            .map_err(|e| LogWatcherError::InitError(format!(\"Failed to create debouncer: {}\", e)))?\n397\t        };\n398\t\n399\t        debouncer\n400\t            .watch(&config.log_dir, RecursiveMode::NonRecursive)\n401\t            .map_err(|e| {\n402\t                LogWatcherError::InitError(format!(\"Failed to watch directory {:?}: {}\", config.log_dir, e))\n403\t            })?;\n404\t\n405\t        let watcher = Self {\n406\t            log_dir: config.log_dir.clone(),\n407\t            parser: LogParser::new(),\n408\t            tracked_files: HashMap::new(),\n409\t            _debouncer: Some(debouncer),\n410\t        };\n411\t\n412\t        Ok((watcher, event_rx))\n413\t    }\n414\t\n415\t    /// Poll for new log content and parse it.\n416\t    ///\n417\t    /// This should be called regularly (e.g., in the TUI event loop).\n418\t    pub fn poll(&mut self) -> Vec<LogWatcherEvent> {\n419\t        let mut events = Vec::new();\n420\t\n421\t        // Check for new log files\n422\t        if let Ok(entries) = std::fs::read_dir(&self.log_dir) {\n423\t            for entry in entries.flatten() {\n424\t                let path = entry.path();\n425\t                if path.extension().and_then(|e| e.to_str()) == Some(\"log\") {\n426\t                    if !self.tracked_files.contains_key(&path) {\n427\t                        // New file discovered\n428\t                        let worker_id = path\n429\t                            .file_stem()\n430\t                            .and_then(|s| s.to_str())\n431\t                            .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n432\t                            .to_string();\n433\t\n434\t                        self.tracked_files.insert(\n435\t                            path.clone(),\n436\t                            TrackedFile {\n437\t                                position: 0,\n438\t                                #[cfg(unix)]\n439\t                                inode: None,\n440\t                                worker_id: worker_id.clone(),\n441\t                            },\n442\t                        );\n443\t\n444\t                        events.push(LogWatcherEvent::FileDiscovered { path, worker_id });\n445\t                    }\n446\t                }\n447\t            }\n448\t        }\n449\t\n450\t        // Collect paths to process (avoid borrow issues)\n451\t        let paths: Vec<PathBuf> = self.tracked_files.keys().cloned().collect();\n452\t\n453\t        // Read new content from tracked files\n454\t        for path in paths {\n455\t            if let Some(tracked) = self.tracked_files.get_mut(&path) {\n456\t                match Self::read_new_content_static(&path, tracked, &self.parser) {\n457\t                    Ok(calls) => {\n458\t                        for call in calls {\n459\t                            events.push(LogWatcherEvent::ApiCallParsed { call });\n460\t                        }\n461\t                    }\n462\t                    Err(e) => {\n463\t                        trace!(\"Error reading log file {:?}: {}\", path, e);\n464\t                    }\n465\t                }\n466\t            }\n467\t        }\n468\t\n469\t        events\n470\t    }\n471\t\n472\t    /// Static version of read_new_content to avoid borrow checker issues.\n473\t    fn read_new_content_static(\n474\t        path: &Path,\n475\t        tracked: &mut TrackedFile,\n476\t        parser: &LogParser,\n477\t    ) -> Result<Vec<ApiCall>, LogWatcherError> {\n478\t        let mut file = File::open(path).map_err(|e| LogWatcherError::ReadError {\n479\t            path: path.to_path_buf(),\n480\t            source: e,\n481\t        })?;\n482\t\n483\t        // Check for rotation (inode change on Unix)\n484\t        #[cfg(unix)]\n485\t        {\n486\t            use std::os::unix::fs::MetadataExt;\n487\t            let metadata = file.metadata().map_err(|e| LogWatcherError::ReadError {\n488\t                path: path.to_path_buf(),\n489\t                source: e,\n490\t            })?;\n491\t            let current_inode = metadata.ino();\n492\t\n493\t            if let Some(prev_inode) = tracked.inode {\n494\t                if current_inode != prev_inode {\n495\t                    // File rotated - reset position\n496\t                    debug!(\"Log file rotated: {:?}\", path);\n497\t                    tracked.position = 0;\n498\t                }\n499\t            }\n500\t            tracked.inode = Some(current_inode);\n501\t        }\n502\t\n503\t        // Seek to last position\n504\t        file.seek(SeekFrom::Start(tracked.position))\n505\t            .map_err(|e| LogWatcherError::ReadError {\n506\t                path: path.to_path_buf(),\n507\t                source: e,\n508\t            })?;\n509\t\n510\t        let mut reader = BufReader::new(file);\n511\t        let mut calls = Vec::new();\n512\t\n513\t        loop {\n514\t            let mut line = String::new();\n515\t            match reader.read_line(&mut line) {\n516\t                Ok(0) => break, // EOF\n517\t                Ok(_) => {\n518\t                    let line = line.trim();\n519\t                    if line.is_empty() || !line.starts_with('{') {\n520\t                        continue;\n521\t                    }\n522\t\n523\t                    // Parse the line for API usage\n524\t                    match parser.parse_line(line, &tracked.worker_id) {\n525\t                        Ok(Some(call)) => {\n526\t                            calls.push(call);\n527\t                        }\n528\t                        Ok(None) => {\n529\t                            // Line didn't contain usage data - skip\n530\t                        }\n531\t                        Err(e) => {\n532\t                            trace!(\"Failed to parse log line: {}\", e);\n533\t                        }\n534\t                    }\n535\t                }\n536\t                Err(e) => {\n537\t                    warn!(\"Error reading line from {:?}: {}\", path, e);\n538\t                    break;\n539\t                }\n540\t            }\n541\t        }\n542\t\n543\t        // Update position\n544\t        tracked.position = reader\n545\t            .stream_position()\n546\t            .map_err(|e| LogWatcherError::ReadError {\n547\t                path: path.to_path_buf(),\n548\t                source: e,\n549\t            })?;\n550\t\n551\t        Ok(calls)\n552\t    }\n553\t\n554\t    /// Get the log directory being watched.\n555\t    pub fn log_dir(&self) -> &Path {\n556\t        &self.log_dir\n557\t    }\n558\t\n559\t    /// Get the number of tracked files.\n560\t    pub fn tracked_file_count(&self) -> usize {\n561\t        self.tracked_files.len()\n562\t    }\n563\t}\n564\t\n565\t/// Simple file tailer for reading new lines from log files.\n566\tpub struct FileTailer {\n567\t    /// Event sender\n568\t    event_tx: Sender<LogWatcherEvent>,\n569\t}\n570\t\n571\timpl FileTailer {\n572\t    /// Create a new file tailer.\n573\t    pub fn new(event_tx: Sender<LogWatcherEvent>) -> Self {\n574\t        Self { event_tx }\n575\t    }\n576\t\n577\t    /// Tail a file, sending events for new lines.\n578\t    pub fn tail_file(&self, path: &Path, position: &mut u64, worker_id: &str) {\n579\t        let file = match File::open(path) {\n580\t            Ok(f) => f,\n581\t            Err(_) => return,\n582\t        };\n583\t\n584\t        let mut reader = BufReader::new(file);\n585\t\n586\t        // Seek to position\n587\t        if reader.seek(SeekFrom::Start(*position)).is_err() {\n588\t            return;\n589\t        }\n590\t\n591\t        let parser = LogParser::new();\n592\t\n593\t        loop {\n594\t            let mut line = String::new();\n595\t            match reader.read_line(&mut line) {\n596\t                Ok(0) => break,\n597\t                Ok(_) => {\n598\t                    let line = line.trim();\n599\t                    if line.is_empty() || !line.starts_with('{') {\n600\t                        continue;\n601\t                    }\n602\t\n603\t                    if let Ok(Some(call)) = parser.parse_line(line, worker_id) {\n604\t                        let _ = self.event_tx.send(LogWatcherEvent::ApiCallParsed { call });\n605\t                    }\n606\t                }\n607\t                Err(_) => break,\n608\t            }\n609\t        }\n610\t\n611\t        // Update position\n612\t        if let Ok(new_pos) = reader.stream_position() {\n613\t            *position = new_pos;\n614\t        }\n615\t    }\n616\t}\n617\t\n618\t/// Process a file system event.\n619\tfn process_file_event(\n620\t    event: &NotifyEvent,\n621\t    _log_dir: &Path,\n622\t    tx: &Sender<LogWatcherEvent>,\n623\t) -> Result<(), LogWatcherError> {\n624\t    for path in &event.paths {\n625\t        // Only process .log files\n626\t        if path.extension().and_then(|e| e.to_str()) != Some(\"log\") {\n627\t            continue;\n628\t        }\n629\t\n630\t        // Extract worker ID from filename\n631\t        let worker_id = match path.file_stem().and_then(|s| s.to_str()) {\n632\t            Some(id) => id.to_string(),\n633\t            None => continue,\n634\t        };\n635\t\n636\t        debug!(\"Processing log event {:?} for worker {}\", event.kind, worker_id);\n637\t\n638\t        match event.kind {\n639\t            EventKind::Create(_) => {\n640\t                let _ = tx.send(LogWatcherEvent::FileDiscovered {\n641\t                    path: path.clone(),\n642\t                    worker_id,\n643\t                });\n644\t            }\n645\t            EventKind::Modify(_) => {\n646\t                // Modifications are handled by polling\n647\t            }\n648\t            EventKind::Remove(_) => {\n649\t                // File removed - this could be rotation\n650\t                let _ = tx.send(LogWatcherEvent::FileRotated {\n651\t                    path: path.clone(),\n652\t                    worker_id,\n653\t                });\n654\t            }\n655\t            _ => {}\n656\t        }\n657\t    }\n658\t\n659\t    Ok(())\n660\t}\n661\t\n662\t/// Real-time metrics aggregator for parsed API calls.\n663\t#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)]\n664\tpub struct RealtimeMetrics {\n665\t    /// Total API calls today\n666\t    pub total_calls: i64,\n667\t\n668\t    /// Total cost today (USD)\n669\t    pub total_cost: f64,\n670\t\n671\t    /// Total input tokens today\n672\t    pub total_input_tokens: i64,\n673\t\n674\t    /// Total output tokens today\n675\t    pub total_output_tokens: i64,\n676\t\n677\t    /// Calls per model\n678\t    pub calls_by_model: HashMap<String, i64>,\n679\t\n680\t    /// Cost per model\n681\t    pub cost_by_model: HashMap<String, f64>,\n682\t\n683\t    /// Last update timestamp\n684\t    pub last_update: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n685\t}\n686\t\n687\timpl RealtimeMetrics {\n688\t    /// Create new empty metrics.\n689\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n690\t        Self::default()\n691\t    }\n692\t\n693\t    /// Record an API call.\n694\t    pub fn record_call(&mut self, call: &ApiCall) {\n695\t        self.total_calls += 1;\n696\t        self.total_cost += call.cost_usd;\n697\t        self.total_input_tokens += call.input_tokens;\n698\t        self.total_output_tokens += call.output_tokens;\n699\t\n700\t        *self.calls_by_model.entry(call.model.clone()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n701\t        *self.cost_by_model.entry(call.model.clone()).or_insert(0.0) += call.cost_usd;\n702\t\n703\t        self.last_update = Some(Utc::now());\n704\t    }\n705\t\n706\t    /// Get average cost per call.\n707\t    pub fn avg_cost_per_call(&self) -> f64 {\n708\t        if self.total_calls == 0 {\n709\t            0.0\n710\t        } else {\n711\t            self.total_cost / self.total_calls as f64\n712\t        }\n713\t    }\n714\t\n715\t    /// Get total tokens.\n716\t    pub fn total_tokens(&self) -> i64 {\n717\t        self.total_input_tokens + self.total_output_tokens\n718\t    }\n719\t\n720\t    /// Clear all metrics.\n721\t    pub fn clear(&mut self) {\n722\t        self.total_calls = 0;\n723\t        self.total_cost = 0.0;\n724\t        self.total_input_tokens = 0;\n725\t        self.total_output_tokens = 0;\n726\t        self.calls_by_model.clear();\n727\t        self.cost_by_model.clear();\n728\t        self.last_update = None;\n729\t    }\n730\t\n731\t    /// Check if we have any data.\n732\t    pub fn has_data(&self) -> bool {\n733\t        self.total_calls > 0\n734\t    }\n735\t}\n736\t\n737\t#[cfg(test)]\n738\tmod Real-time log watching for API usage metrics extraction.\n//!\n//! This module provides file watching and parsing for worker log files\n//! to extract API usage metrics (token counts, costs, model info) in real-time.\n//!\n//! ## Architecture\n//!\n//! - Uses `notify` crate for file system watching with debouncing\n//! - Incremental parsing with file position tracking for efficiency\n//! - Handles log rotation via inode tracking\n//! - Emits parsed API calls via channel for TUI integration\n//!\n//! ## Example\n//!\n//! ```no_run\n//! use forge_tui::log_watcher::{LogWatcher, LogWatcherConfig};\n//!\n//! fn main() {\n//!     let config = LogWatcherConfig::default();\n//!     let (watcher, rx) = LogWatcher::new(config).unwrap();\n//!\n//!     while let Ok(event) = rx.recv() {\n//!         match event {\n//!             forge_tui::log_watcher::LogWatcherEvent::ApiCallParsed { call } => {\n//!                 println!(\"API call: {} tokens, cost ${:.4}\",\n//!                     call.input_tokens + call.output_tokens,\n//!                     call.cost_usd);\n//!             }\n//!             _ => {}\n//!         }\n//!     }\n//! }\n//! ```\n\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse forge_cost::{ApiCall, LogParser};\nuse forge_core::worker_perf::TaskEvent;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse notify::{Event as NotifyEvent, EventKind, RecursiveMode};\nuse notify_debouncer_full::{DebounceEventResult, new_debouncer_opt};\n\n// In tests, use PollWatcher to avoid inotify instance limits.\n// In production, use RecommendedWatcher (inotify on Linux) for efficiency.\n#[cfg(test)]\nuse notify::PollWatcher;\n#[cfg(test)]\nuse notify_debouncer_full::NoCache;\n#[cfg(test)]\ntype DebouncerType = notify_debouncer_full::Debouncer<PollWatcher, NoCache>;\n\n#[cfg(not(test))]\nuse notify::RecommendedWatcher;\n#[cfg(not(test))]\nuse notify_debouncer_full::RecommendedCache;\n#[cfg(not(test))]\ntype DebouncerType = notify_debouncer_full::Debouncer<RecommendedWatcher, RecommendedCache>;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::fs::File;\nuse std::io::{BufRead, BufReader, Seek, SeekFrom};\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\nuse std::sync::mpsc::{self, Sender};\nuse std::time::Duration;\nuse thiserror::Error;\nuse tracing::{debug, trace, warn};\n\n/// Default log directory (~/.forge/logs/).\npub const DEFAULT_LOG_DIR: &str = \".forge/logs\";\n\n/// Default debounce duration in milliseconds.\npub const DEFAULT_DEBOUNCE_MS: u64 = 100;\n\n/// Default poll interval for file reading in milliseconds.\npub const DEFAULT_POLL_INTERVAL_MS: u64 = 500;\n\n/// Default channel buffer size.\npub const DEFAULT_CHANNEL_BUFFER: usize = 256;\n\n/// Errors that can occur during log watching.\n#[derive(Error, Debug)]\npub enum LogWatcherError {\n    /// Failed to initialize watcher\n    #[error(\"Failed to initialize log watcher: {0}\")]\n    InitError(String),\n\n    /// Failed to read log file\n    #[error(\"Failed to read log file {path}: {source}\")]\n    ReadError {\n        path: PathBuf,\n        #[source]\n        source: std::io::Error,\n    },\n\n    /// Failed to parse log entry\n    #[error(\"Failed to parse log entry: {0}\")]\n    ParseError(String),\n\n    /// Channel error\n    #[error(\"Channel error: {0}\")]\n    ChannelError(String),\n}\n\n/// Events emitted by the log watcher.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub enum LogWatcherEvent {\n    /// An API call was parsed from the logs\n    ApiCallParsed { call: ApiCall },\n\n    /// A new log file was discovered\n    FileDiscovered { path: PathBuf, worker_id: String },\n\n    /// A log file was rotated\n    FileRotated { path: PathBuf, worker_id: String },\n\n    /// An error occurred\n    Error { message: String },\n}\n\n/// Configuration for the log watcher.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct LogWatcherConfig {\n    /// Directory to watch for log files\n    pub log_dir: PathBuf,\n\n    /// Debounce duration for file system events\n    pub debounce_duration: Duration,\n\n    /// Poll interval for reading new content\n    pub poll_interval: Duration,\n\n    /// Channel buffer size\n    pub channel_buffer: usize,\n\n    /// Whether to parse existing log files on startup\n    pub parse_existing: bool,\n}\n\nimpl Default for LogWatcherConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        let home = std::env::var(\"HOME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"/tmp\".to_string());\n        Self {\n            log_dir: PathBuf::from(home).join(DEFAULT_LOG_DIR),\n            debounce_duration: Duration::from_millis(DEFAULT_DEBOUNCE_MS),\n            poll_interval: Duration::from_millis(DEFAULT_POLL_INTERVAL_MS),\n            channel_buffer: DEFAULT_CHANNEL_BUFFER,\n            parse_existing: false,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl LogWatcherConfig {\n    /// Create a new config with the given log directory.\n    pub fn new(log_dir: PathBuf) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            log_dir,\n            ..Default::default()\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set the debounce duration.\n    pub fn with_debounce(mut self, duration: Duration) -> Self {\n        self.debounce_duration = duration;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the poll interval.\n    pub fn with_poll_interval(mut self, interval: Duration) -> Self {\n        self.poll_interval = interval;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set whether to parse existing files on startup.\n    pub fn with_parse_existing(mut self, parse: bool) -> Self {\n        self.parse_existing = parse;\n        self\n    }\n}\n\n/// Parse task-related events from log lines for performance tracking.\n///\n/// This function extracts task lifecycle events (start, complete, fail)\n/// from worker log entries to enable performance metrics tracking.\npub fn parse_task_event(line: &str, worker_id: &str) -> Option<TaskEvent> {\n    // Skip non-JSON lines\n    if !line.starts_with('{') {\n        return None;\n    }\n\n    let value: Value = match serde_json::from_str(line) {\n        Ok(v) => v,\n        Err(_) => return None,\n    };\n\n    // Check event type\n    let event_type = value.get(\"type\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or(\"\");\n\n    match event_type {\n        // Task start event - look for tool_use or task initiation\n        \"tool_use\" | \"task_start\" => {\n            let task_id = value.get(\"bead_id\")\n                .or_else(|| value.get(\"task_id\"))\n                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n                .to_string();\n\n            let model = value.get(\"model\")\n                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n                .to_string();\n\n            Some(TaskEvent::Started {\n                task_id,\n                worker_id: worker_id.to_string(),\n                model,\n            })\n        }\n\n        // Task completion event - result type with success/failure\n        \"result\" => {\n            let task_id = value.get(\"bead_id\")\n                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n                .to_string();\n\n            // Check for success/failure\n            let subtype = value.get(\"subtype\")\n                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                .unwrap_or(\"\");\n\n            let success = subtype == \"success\";\n\n            // Extract duration if available\n            let duration_ms = value.get(\"duration_ms\")\n                .or_else(|| value.get(\"duration_api_ms\"))\n                .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n                .unwrap_or(0);\n\n            // Extract usage data\n            let usage = value.get(\"usage\");\n            let (input_tokens, output_tokens) = if let Some(u) = usage {\n                let input = u.get(\"input_tokens\").and_then(|v| v.as_i64()).unwrap_or(0);\n                let output = u.get(\"output_tokens\").and_then(|v| v.as_i64()).unwrap_or(0);\n                (input, output)\n            } else {\n                (0, 0)\n            };\n\n            // Extract or calculate cost\n            let cost_usd = value.get(\"total_cost_usd\")\n                .and_then(|v| v.as_f64())\n                .or_else(|| {\n                    // Try to calculate from usage if pricing available\n                    if input_tokens > 0 || output_tokens > 0 {\n                        // Rough estimate: Opus pricing\n                        let cost = (input_tokens as f64 * 15.0 / 1_000_000.0)\n                            + (output_tokens as f64 * 75.0 / 1_000_000.0);\n                        Some(cost)\n                    } else {\n                        None\n                    }\n                })\n                .unwrap_or(0.0);\n\n            if success {\n                Some(TaskEvent::Completed {\n                    task_id,\n                    worker_id: worker_id.to_string(),\n                    success: true,\n                    duration_ms,\n                    input_tokens,\n                    output_tokens,\n                    cost_usd,\n                })\n            } else {\n                Some(TaskEvent::Failed {\n                    task_id,\n                    worker_id: worker_id.to_string(),\n                    error: subtype.to_string(),\n                })\n            }\n        }\n\n        _ => None,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Tracked file state for incremental parsing.\n#[derive(Debug)]\nstruct TrackedFile {\n    /// Current file position (bytes read so far)\n    position: u64,\n\n    /// File inode for rotation detection (Unix only)\n    #[cfg(unix)]\n    inode: Option<u64>,\n\n    /// Worker ID extracted from filename\n    worker_id: String,\n}\n\n/// Real-time log watcher that monitors log files and parses API usage metrics.\npub struct LogWatcher {\n    /// Log directory being watched\n    log_dir: PathBuf,\n\n    /// Log parser for extracting API calls\n    parser: LogParser,\n\n    /// Tracked files with their positions\n    tracked_files: HashMap<PathBuf, TrackedFile>,\n\n    /// The debounced file watcher (stored for proper cleanup)\n    _debouncer: Option<DebouncerType>,\n}\n\nimpl LogWatcher {\n    /// Create a new log watcher with default configuration.\n    pub fn new(config: LogWatcherConfig) -> Result<(Self, mpsc::Receiver<LogWatcherEvent>), LogWatcherError> {\n        // Create the log directory if it doesn't exist\n        if !config.log_dir.exists() {\n            std::fs::create_dir_all(&config.log_dir).map_err(|e| {\n                LogWatcherError::InitError(format!(\n                    \"Failed to create log directory {:?}: {}\",\n                    config.log_dir, e\n                ))\n            })?;\n        }\n\n        let (event_tx, event_rx) = mpsc::channel();\n\n        // Start file system watcher\n        // In tests: use PollWatcher to avoid inotify instance limits\n        // In production: use RecommendedWatcher (inotify on Linux) for efficiency\n        let log_dir_clone = config.log_dir.clone();\n        let event_tx_clone = event_tx.clone();\n\n        #[cfg(test)]\n        let mut debouncer = {\n            // Use PollWatcher in tests to avoid inotify instance limits\n            // Configure short poll interval and content comparison for reliable test behavior\n            let poll_interval = config.poll_interval;\n            new_debouncer_opt::<_, PollWatcher, _>(\n                config.debounce_duration,\n                None,\n                move |result: DebounceEventResult| {\n                    match result {\n                        Ok(events) => {\n                            for event in events {\n                                if let Err(e) = process_file_event(&event.event, &log_dir_clone, &event_tx_clone) {\n                                    let _ = event_tx_clone.send(LogWatcherEvent::Error {\n                                        message: e.to_string(),\n                                    });\n                                }\n                            }\n                        }\n                        Err(errors) => {\n                            for error in errors {\n                                warn!(\"File watcher error: {:?}\", error);\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                },\n                NoCache::new(),\n                notify::Config::default()\n                    .with_poll_interval(poll_interval)\n                    .with_compare_contents(true),\n            )\n            .map_err(|e| LogWatcherError::InitError(format!(\"Failed to create debouncer: {}\", e)))?\n        };\n\n        #[cfg(not(test))]\n        let mut debouncer = {\n            new_debouncer_opt::<_, RecommendedWatcher, _>(\n                config.debounce_duration,\n                None,\n                move |result: DebounceEventResult| {\n                    match result {\n                        Ok(events) => {\n                            for event in events {\n                                if let Err(e) = process_file_event(&event.event, &log_dir_clone, &event_tx_clone) {\n                                    let _ = event_tx_clone.send(LogWatcherEvent::Error {\n                                        message: e.to_string(),\n                                    });\n                                }\n                            }\n                        }\n                        Err(errors) => {\n                            for error in errors {\n                                warn!(\"File watcher error: {:?}\", error);\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                },\n                RecommendedCache::new(),\n                notify::Config::default(),\n            )\n            .map_err(|e| LogWatcherError::InitError(format!(\"Failed to create debouncer: {}\", e)))?\n        };\n\n        debouncer\n            .watch(&config.log_dir, RecursiveMode::NonRecursive)\n            .map_err(|e| {\n                LogWatcherError::InitError(format!(\"Failed to watch directory {:?}: {}\", config.log_dir, e))\n            })?;\n\n        let watcher = Self {\n            log_dir: config.log_dir.clone(),\n            parser: LogParser::new(),\n            tracked_files: HashMap::new(),\n            _debouncer: Some(debouncer),\n        };\n\n        Ok((watcher, event_rx))\n    }\n\n    /// Poll for new log content and parse it.\n    ///\n    /// This should be called regularly (e.g., in the TUI event loop).\n    pub fn poll(&mut self) -> Vec<LogWatcherEvent> {\n        let mut events = Vec::new();\n\n        // Check for new log files\n        if let Ok(entries) = std::fs::read_dir(&self.log_dir) {\n            for entry in entries.flatten() {\n                let path = entry.path();\n                if path.extension().and_then(|e| e.to_str()) == Some(\"log\") {\n                    if !self.tracked_files.contains_key(&path) {\n                        // New file discovered\n                        let worker_id = path\n                            .file_stem()\n                            .and_then(|s| s.to_str())\n                            .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n                            .to_string();\n\n                        self.tracked_files.insert(\n                            path.clone(),\n                            TrackedFile {\n                                position: 0,\n                                #[cfg(unix)]\n                                inode: None,\n                                worker_id: worker_id.clone(),\n                            },\n                        );\n\n                        events.push(LogWatcherEvent::FileDiscovered { path, worker_id });\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Collect paths to process (avoid borrow issues)\n        let paths: Vec<PathBuf> = self.tracked_files.keys().cloned().collect();\n\n        // Read new content from tracked files\n        for path in paths {\n            if let Some(tracked) = self.tracked_files.get_mut(&path) {\n                match Self::read_new_content_static(&path, tracked, &self.parser) {\n                    Ok(calls) => {\n                        for call in calls {\n                            events.push(LogWatcherEvent::ApiCallParsed { call });\n                        }\n                    }\n                    Err(e) => {\n                        trace!(\"Error reading log file {:?}: {}\", path, e);\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        events\n    }\n\n    /// Static version of read_new_content to avoid borrow checker issues.\n    fn read_new_content_static(\n        path: &Path,\n        tracked: &mut TrackedFile,\n        parser: &LogParser,\n    ) -> Result<Vec<ApiCall>, LogWatcherError> {\n        let mut file = File::open(path).map_err(|e| LogWatcherError::ReadError {\n            path: path.to_path_buf(),\n            source: e,\n        })?;\n\n        // Check for rotation (inode change on Unix)\n        #[cfg(unix)]\n        {\n            use std::os::unix::fs::MetadataExt;\n            let metadata = file.metadata().map_err(|e| LogWatcherError::ReadError {\n                path: path.to_path_buf(),\n                source: e,\n            })?;\n            let current_inode = metadata.ino();\n\n            if let Some(prev_inode) = tracked.inode {\n                if current_inode != prev_inode {\n                    // File rotated - reset position\n                    debug!(\"Log file rotated: {:?}\", path);\n                    tracked.position = 0;\n                }\n            }\n            tracked.inode = Some(current_inode);\n        }\n\n        // Seek to last position\n        file.seek(SeekFrom::Start(tracked.position))\n            .map_err(|e| LogWatcherError::ReadError {\n                path: path.to_path_buf(),\n                source: e,\n            })?;\n\n        let mut reader = BufReader::new(file);\n        let mut calls = Vec::new();\n\n        loop {\n            let mut line = String::new();\n            match reader.read_line(&mut line) {\n                Ok(0) => break, // EOF\n                Ok(_) => {\n                    let line = line.trim();\n                    if line.is_empty() || !line.starts_with('{') {\n                        continue;\n                    }\n\n                    // Parse the line for API usage\n                    match parser.parse_line(line, &tracked.worker_id) {\n                        Ok(Some(call)) => {\n                            calls.push(call);\n                        }\n                        Ok(None) => {\n                            // Line didn't contain usage data - skip\n                        }\n                        Err(e) => {\n                            trace!(\"Failed to parse log line: {}\", e);\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    warn!(\"Error reading line from {:?}: {}\", path, e);\n                    break;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Update position\n        tracked.position = reader\n            .stream_position()\n            .map_err(|e| LogWatcherError::ReadError {\n                path: path.to_path_buf(),\n                source: e,\n            })?;\n\n        Ok(calls)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the log directory being watched.\n    pub fn log_dir(&self) -> &Path {\n        &self.log_dir\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of tracked files.\n    pub fn tracked_file_count(&self) -> usize {\n        self.tracked_files.len()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Simple file tailer for reading new lines from log files.\npub struct FileTailer {\n    /// Event sender\n    event_tx: Sender<LogWatcherEvent>,\n}\n\nimpl FileTailer {\n    /// Create a new file tailer.\n    pub fn new(event_tx: Sender<LogWatcherEvent>) -> Self {\n        Self { event_tx }\n    }\n\n    /// Tail a file, sending events for new lines.\n    pub fn tail_file(&self, path: &Path, position: &mut u64, worker_id: &str) {\n        let file = match File::open(path) {\n            Ok(f) => f,\n            Err(_) => return,\n        };\n\n        let mut reader = BufReader::new(file);\n\n        // Seek to position\n        if reader.seek(SeekFrom::Start(*position)).is_err() {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        let parser = LogParser::new();\n\n        loop {\n            let mut line = String::new();\n            match reader.read_line(&mut line) {\n                Ok(0) => break,\n                Ok(_) => {\n                    let line = line.trim();\n                    if line.is_empty() || !line.starts_with('{') {\n                        continue;\n                    }\n\n                    if let Ok(Some(call)) = parser.parse_line(line, worker_id) {\n                        let _ = self.event_tx.send(LogWatcherEvent::ApiCallParsed { call });\n                    }\n                }\n                Err(_) => break,\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Update position\n        if let Ok(new_pos) = reader.stream_position() {\n            *position = new_pos;\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Process a file system event.\nfn process_file_event(\n    event: &NotifyEvent,\n    _log_dir: &Path,\n    tx: &Sender<LogWatcherEvent>,\n) -> Result<(), LogWatcherError> {\n    for path in &event.paths {\n        // Only process .log files\n        if path.extension().and_then(|e| e.to_str()) != Some(\"log\") {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // Extract worker ID from filename\n        let worker_id = match path.file_stem().and_then(|s| s.to_str()) {\n            Some(id) => id.to_string(),\n            None => continue,\n        };\n\n        debug!(\"Processing log event {:?} for worker {}\", event.kind, worker_id);\n\n        match event.kind {\n            EventKind::Create(_) => {\n                let _ = tx.send(LogWatcherEvent::FileDiscovered {\n                    path: path.clone(),\n                    worker_id,\n                });\n            }\n            EventKind::Modify(_) => {\n                // Modifications are handled by polling\n            }\n            EventKind::Remove(_) => {\n                // File removed - this could be rotation\n                let _ = tx.send(LogWatcherEvent::FileRotated {\n                    path: path.clone(),\n                    worker_id,\n                });\n            }\n            _ => {}\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// Real-time metrics aggregator for parsed API calls.\n#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)]\npub struct RealtimeMetrics {\n    /// Total API calls today\n    pub total_calls: i64,\n\n    /// Total cost today (USD)\n    pub total_cost: f64,\n\n    /// Total input tokens today\n    pub total_input_tokens: i64,\n\n    /// Total output tokens today\n    pub total_output_tokens: i64,\n\n    /// Calls per model\n    pub calls_by_model: HashMap<String, i64>,\n\n    /// Cost per model\n    pub cost_by_model: HashMap<String, f64>,\n\n    /// Last update timestamp\n    pub last_update: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n}\n\nimpl RealtimeMetrics {\n    /// Create new empty metrics.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self::default()\n    }\n\n    /// Record an API call.\n    pub fn record_call(&mut self, call: &ApiCall) {\n        self.total_calls += 1;\n        self.total_cost += call.cost_usd;\n        self.total_input_tokens += call.input_tokens;\n        self.total_output_tokens += call.output_tokens;\n\n        *self.calls_by_model.entry(call.model.clone()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n        *self.cost_by_model.entry(call.model.clone()).or_insert(0.0) += call.cost_usd;\n\n        self.last_update = Some(Utc::now());\n    }\n\n    /// Get average cost per call.\n    pub fn avg_cost_per_call(&self) -> f64 {\n        if self.total_calls == 0 {\n            0.0\n        } else {\n            self.total_cost / self.total_calls as f64\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get total tokens.\n    pub fn total_tokens(&self) -> i64 {\n        self.total_input_tokens + self.total_output_tokens\n    }\n\n    /// Clear all metrics.\n    pub fn clear(&mut self) {\n        self.total_calls = 0;\n        self.total_cost = 0.0;\n        self.total_input_tokens = 0;\n        self.total_output_tokens = 0;\n        self.calls_by_model.clear();\n        self.cost_by_model.clear();\n        self.last_update = None;\n    }\n\n    /// Check if we have any data.\n    pub fn has_data(&self) -> bool {\n        self.total_calls > 0\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use serial_test::serial;\n    use std::fs;\n    use std::io::Write;\n    use tempfile::TempDir;\n\n    #[test]\n   }\n167\t\n168\t    /// Get tasks per hour for today.\n169\t    pub fn tasks_per_hour(&self) -> f64 {\n170\t        self.today\n171\t            .as_ref()\n172\t            .map(|t| {\n173\t                let total_tasks = t.tasks_completed + t.tasks_failed;\n174\t                if total_tasks == 0 {\n175\t                    0.0\n176\t                } else {\n177\t                    // Assume 24 hours for daily stats\n178\t                    total_tasks as f64 / 24.0\n179\t                }\n180\t            })\n181\t            .unwrap_or(0.0)\n182\t    }\n183\t\n184\t    /// Check if we have any data.\n185\t    pub fn has_data(&self) -> bool {\n186\t        self.today.is_some()\n187\t            || !self.hourly_stats.is_empty()\n188\t            || !self.model_performance.is_empty()\n189\t            || !self.worker_efficiency.is_empty()\n190\t            || self.realtime.has_data()\n191\t            || !self.task_trend_7day.is_empty()\n192\t            || !self.cost_trend_7day.is_empty()\n193\t            || !self.tasks_per_hour.is_empty()\n194\t            || !self.model_performance_7day.is_empty()\n195\t            || !self.worker_efficiency_7day.is_empty()\n196\t    }\n197\t}\n198\t\n199\t/// Renders a sparkline from a series of values.\n200\tpub fn render_sparkline(values: &[i64], width: usize) -> String {\n201\t    if values.is_empty() {\n202\t        return \" \".repeat(width);\n203\t    }\n204\t\n205\t    let blocks = ['▁', '▂', '▃', '▄', '▅', '▆', '▇', '█'];\n206\t    let max = values.iter().cloned().fold(0i64, i64::max);\n207\t    let min = values.iter().cloned().fold(i64::MAX, i64::min);\n208\t    let range = (max - min) as f64;\n209\t\n210\t    // Sample values to fit width\n211\t    let step = values.len() as f64 / width as f64;\n212\t    let mut result = String::with_capacity(width);\n213\t\n214\t    for i in 0..width {\n215\t        let idx = ((i as f64) * step).min(values.len() as f64 - 1.0) as usize;\n216\t        let val = values[idx];\n217\t\n218\t        let normalized = if range > 0.0 {\n219\t            ((val - min) as f64 / range).clamp(0.0, 1.0)\n220\t        } else {\n221\t            0.5\n222\t        };\n223\t\n224\t        let block_idx = ((normalized * 7.0).round() as usize).min(7);\n225\t        result.push(blocks[block_idx]);\n226\t    }\n227\t\n228\t    result\n229\t}\n230\t\n231\t/// Renders a horizontal bar chart.\n232\tpub fn render_bar(\n233\t    value: f64,\n234\t    max: f64,\n235\t    width: usize,\n236\t    filled_char: char,\n237\t    empty_char: char,\n238\t) -> String {\n239\t    let pct = if max > 0.0 {\n240\t        (value / max).clamp(0.0, 1.0)\n241\t    } else {\n242\t        0.0\n243\t    };\n244\t    let filled = (pct * width as f64).round() as usize;\n245\t    let empty = width.saturating_sub(filled);\n246\t\n247\t    format!(\n248\t        \"{}{}\",\n249\t        filled_char.to_string().repeat(filled),\n250\t        empty_char.to_string().repeat(empty)\n251\t    )\n252\t}\n253\t\n254\t/// Format a duration in seconds to human-readable form.\n255\tpub fn format_duration(secs: i64) -> String {\n256\t    if secs < 60 {\n257\t        format!(\"{}s\", secs)\n258\t    } else if secs < 3600 {\n259\t        let mins = secs / 60;\n260\t        let remaining_secs = secs % 60;\n261\t        format!(\"{}m {}s\", mins, remaining_secs)\n262\t    } else {\n263\t        let hours = secs / 3600;\n264\t        let mins = (secs % 3600) / 60;\n265\t        format!(\"{}h {}m\", hours, mins)\n266\t    }\n267\t}\n268\t\n269\t/// Format a dollar amount for display.\n270\tpub fn format_usd(amount: f64) -> String {\n271\t    if amount >= 1000.0 {\n272\t        format!(\"${:.2}K\", amount / 1000.0)\n273\t    } else if amount >= 100.0 {\n274\t        format!(\"${:.1}\", amount)\n275\t    } else if amount >= 10.0 {\n276\t        format!(\"${:.2}\", amount)\n277\t    } else if amount >= 1.0 {\n278\t        format!(\"${:.3}\", amount)\n279\t    } else if amount > 0.0 {\n280\t        format!(\"${:.4}\", amount)\n281\t    } else {\n282\t        \"$0.00\".to_string()\n283\t    }\n284\t}\n285\t\n286\t/// Truncate model name for display.\n287\tpub fn truncate_model_name(model: &str, max_len: usize) -> String {\n288\t    // Common abbreviations\n289\t    let name = model\n290\t        .replace(\"claude-opus-4-5-20251101\", \"Opus-4.5\")\n291\t        .replace(\"claude-opus-4-6\", \"Opus-4.6\")\n292\t        .replace(\"claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929\", \"Sonnet-4.5\")\n293\t        .replace(\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\", \"Haiku-4.5\")\n294\t        .replace(\"glm-4.7\", \"GLM-4.7\")\n295\t        .replace(\"deepseek-\", \"DS-\");\n296\t\n297\t    if name.len() <= max_len {\n298\t        name\n299\t    } else if max_len > 2 {\n300\t        format!(\"{}…\", &name[..max_len - 1])\n301\t    } else {\n302\t        name[..max_len].to_string()\n303\t    }\n304\t}\n305\t\n306\t/// Performance metrics panel widget.\n307\tpub struct MetricsPanel<'a> {\n308\t    data: &'a MetricsPanelData,\n309\t    focused: bool,\n310\t}\n311\t\n312\timpl<'a> MetricsPanel<'a> {\n313\t    /// Create a new metrics panel.\n314\t    pub fn new(data: &'a MetricsPanelData) -> Self {\n315\t        Self {\n316\t            data,\n317\t            focused: false,\n318\t        }\n319\t    }\n320\t\n321\t    /// Set focus state.\n322\t    pub fn focused(mut self, focused: bool) -> Self {\n323\t        self.focused = focused;\n324\t        self\n325\t    }\n326\t\n327\t    /// Render the summary section.\n328\t    fn render_summary(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {\n329\t        let mut lines = total\"),\n358\t        ]));\n359\t\n360\t        lines.push(Line::from(vec![\n361\t            Span::raw(\"Success: \"),\n362\t            Span::styled(\n363\t                format!(\"{:.1}%\", ]));\n388\t\n389\t        // Real-time API call metrics from log parsing\n390\t        if self.data.realtime.has_data() {\n391\t            lines.push(Line::from(\"\"));\n392\t            lines.push(Line::from(vec![\n393\t                Span::styled(\"⚡ Live\", Style::default().fg(Color::Magenta).add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD)),\n394\t                Span::raw(\" \"),\n395\t                Span::styled(\n396\t                    format!(\"{} API calls\", self.data.realtime.total_calls),\n397\t                    Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan),\n398\t                ),\n399\t            ]));\n400\t            lines.push(Line::from(vec![\n401\t                Span::raw(\"   Tokens: \"),\n402\t                Span::styled(\n403\t                    format!(\"{}\", Span::styled(\n408\t                    format_usd(self.data.realtime.total_cost),\n409\t                    Style::default().fg(Color::Green),\n410\t                ),\n411\t            ]));\n412\t        }\n413\t\n414\t        let paragraph = Paragraph::new(lines);\n415\t        paragraph.render(area, buf);\n416\t    }\n417\t\n418\t    /// Render the tasks per hour histogram.\n419\t    fn      .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),\n433\t            ),\n434\t            Span::raw(\" (last 24h)\"),\n435\t        ]));\n436\t        lines.push(Line::from(\"\"));\n437\t\n438\t        // Get tasks       .collect();\n445\t\n446\t        let max_val = values.iter().cloned().fold(0i64, i64::max);\n447\t\n448\t        // Render horizontal histogram with bars\n449\t        for (i, &val) in Color::DarkGray\n463\t            } else if val >= (max_val / 2) {\n464\t                Color::Green\n465\t            } else {\n466\t                Color::Cyan\n467\t            };\n468\t\n469\t            lines.push(Line::from(vec![\n470\t                Span::raw(hour_label),\n471\t                Span::raw(\" \"),\n472\t                Span::styled(bar, Style::default().fg(bar_color)),\n473\t                Span::styled(format!(\" {:>3}\", val), Style::default().fg(Color::Gray)),\n474\t            ]));\n475\t        }\n476\t\n477\t        let paragraph = Paragraph::new(lines);\n478\t        paragraph.render(area, buf);\n479\t    }\n480\t\n481\t    /// Render the model efficiency comparison table with 5+ metrics.\n482\t    fn render_model_comparison(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {\n483\t        // Use 7-day aggregated data if available\n484\t        let models = if !self.data.model_performance_7day.is_empty() {\n485\t            &self.data.model_performance_7day\n486\t        } else {\n487\t            &self.data.model_performance\n488\t        };\n489\t\n490\t        if models.is_empty() {\n491\t            let msg = Paragraph::new(\"No model expensive models first\n497\t        let mut sorted_models = models.clone();\n498\t        sorted_models.sort_by(|a, b| .take(6)\n503\t            .map(|m| {\n504\t                let success_pct = (m.success_rate * 100.0) as i64;\n505\t\n506\t                // Format cache hit rate\n507\t                let cache_pct format_usd(m.avg_cost_per_task),\n515\t                    format!(\"{:.0}%\", cache_pct),\n516\t                ])\n517\t            })\n518\t            .collect();\n519\t\n520\t        let header = Row::new(vec![\"Model\", \"Calls\", \"Done\", \"Success\", \"Cost/T\", \"Cache\"])\n521\t            .style(\n522\t                Style::default()\n523\t                    .fg(Color::Gray)\n524\t                    .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),\n525\t            )\n526\t            .bottom_margin(0);\n527\t\n528\t        let widths = [\n529\t            Constraint::Length(10),\n530\t            Constraint::Length(5),\n531\t            Constraint::Length(4),\n532\t            Constraint::Length(6),\n533\t            Constraint::Length(7),\n534\t            Constraint::Length(5),\n535\t        ];\n536\t\n537\t        let table = Table::new(rows, widths).header(header).column_spacing(1);\n538\t\n539\t        ratatui::widgets::Widget::render(table, area, buf);\n540\t    }\n541\t\n542\t    /// Render the trend sparkline.\n543\t    fn render_trend(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {\n544\t        if self.data.hourly_stats.is_empty() {\n545\t            let msg = Paragraph::new(\"No trend Style::default().fg(Color::Green),\n567\t        )));\n568\t\n569\t        let min_val = values.iter().cloned().fold(i64::MAX, i64::min);\n570\t        let max_val = values.iter().cloned().fold(0i64,     Span::styled(format!(\"{}\", max_val), Style::default().fg(Color::White)),\n575\t        ]));\n576\t\n577\t        let paragraph = Paragraph::new(lines);\n578\t        paragraph.render(area, buf);\n579\t    }\n580\t\n581\t    /// Render the 7-day trend sparkline.\n582\t    fn render_7day_trend(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {\n583\t        // Use the new 7-day trend data if available, otherwise fall back to hourly\n584\t        let values = if !self.data.task_trend_7day.is_empty() {\n585\t            self.data.task_trend_7day.clone()\n586\t        } else {\n587\t            self.data\n588\t                .hourly_stats\n589\t                .iter()\n590\t                .map(|h| h.tasks_completed)\n591\t                .collect()\n592\t        };\n593\t\n594\t return;\n598\t        }\n599\t\n600\t        let sparkline = render_sparkline(&values, area.width.saturating_sub(14) as usize);\n601\t\n602\t        let total: i64 = values.iter().sum();\n603\t lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(\n624\t            sparkline,\n625\t            Style::default().fg(Color::Green),\n626\t        )));\n627\t\n628\t        let paragraph = &self.data.worker_efficiency\n638\t        };\n639\t\n640\t        if workers.is_empty() {\n641\t            let msg = Paragraph::new(\"No worker {\n715\t        // Enhanced focus indicator: \"▶\" arrow for focused (clearer than diamond), \"▪\" for unfocused\n716\t        let focus_icon = if self.focused { \"▶\" } else { \"▪\" };\n717\t\n718\t        // Border type: Double border for focused (highly visible), Normal for unfocused\n719\t        let border_type = if self.focused {\n720\t            BorderType::Double\n721\t        } else {\n722\t            BorderType::Plain\n723\t        };\n724\t\n725\t        // Border style: bright cyan with bold for focused, dim for unfocused\n726\t        let border_style = if self.focused {\n727\t            Style::default()\n728\t                .fg(Color::Cyan)\n729\t                .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD)\n730\t        } else {\n731\t            Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)\n732\t        };\n733\t\n734\t        // Title style: bold + underlined for focused, dim for unfocused\n735\t        let title_style = if self.focused {\n736\t            Style::default()\n737\t                .fg(Color::Cyan)\n738\t                .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD | Modifier::UNDERLINED)\n739\t        } else {\n740\t            Style::default().fg(Color::Rgb(80, 80, 80))\n741\t        };\n742\t\n743\t        let block = Block::default()\n744\t            .borders(Borders::ALL)\n745\t            .border_type(border_type)\n746\t            .border_style(border_style)\n747\t            .title(Span::styled(\n748\t                format!(\" {} Performance Analytics \", focus_icon),\n749\t                title_style,\n750\t            ));\n751\t\n752\t        let inner = block.inner(area);\n753\t        block.render(area, buf);\n754\t\n755\t        // Handle special states\n756\t        if self.data.is_loading {\n757\t            let loading =\n758\t                Paragraph::new(\"Loading metrics...\").style(Style::default().fg(Color::Yellow));\n759\t            loading.render(inner, let no_data = Paragraph::new(\n772\t                \"No performance data available.\\n\\n\\\n773\t                 Metrics tracking requires:\\n\\\n774\t                 - forge-cost database .style(Style::default().fg(Color::Gray));\n779\t            no_data.render(inner, buf);\n780\t            return;\n781\t        }\n782\t\n783\t        // Determine layout based on available
