Performance metrics visualization panel for the FORGE TUI.\n//!\n//! This module provides rich performance visualization with:\n//! - Summary statistics (tasks completed, avg duration, success rate)\n//! - Tasks per hour histogram (24-hour bar chart)\n//! - Model efficiency comparison table (5+ metrics)\n//! - Trend sparklines (7-day trend)\n//! - Worker efficiency ranking (sorted by tasks/hour)\n//! - Cost efficiency by priority level\n//! - Color-coded performance indicators\n\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse ratatui::{\n    buffer::Buffer,\n    layout::{Constraint, Direction, Layout, Rect},\n    style::{Color, Modifier, Style},\n    text::{Line, Span},\n    widgets::{Block, BorderType, Borders, Paragraph, Row, Table, Widget},\n};\n\nuse crate::log_watcher::RealtimeMetrics;\nuse forge_cost::{DailyStat, HourlyStat, ModelPerformance, WorkerEfficiency};\n\n/// Performance data prepared for TUI display.\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\npub struct MetricsPanelData {\n    /// Today's performance statistics\n    pub today: Option<DailyStat>,\n    /// Hourly statistics for the last 24 hours\n    pub hourly_stats: Vec<HourlyStat>,\n    /// Model performance comparison\n    pub model_performance: Vec<ModelPerformance>,\n    /// Worker efficiency ranking\n    pub worker_efficiency: Vec<WorkerEfficiency>,\n    /// Real-time metrics from log parsing\n    pub realtime: RealtimeMetrics,\n    /// Last update timestamp\n    pub last_update: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n    /// Whether data is loading\n    pub is_loading: bool,\n    /// Error message if any\n    pub error: Option<String>,\n    // Enhanced analytics data\n    /// 7-day task completion trend (for sparkline)\n    pub task_trend_7day: Vec<i64>,\n    /// 7-day cost trend (for sparkline)\n    pub cost_trend_7day: Vec<f64>,\n    /// Tasks per hour for last 24 hours (for histogram)\n    pub tasks_per_hour: Vec<i64>,\n    /// Model performance aggregated over 7 days\n    pub model_performance_7day: Vec<ModelPerformance>,\n    /// Worker efficiency aggregated over 7 days\n    pub worker_efficiency_7day: Vec<WorkerEfficiency>,\n    /// Average cost per task by model\n    pub avg_cost_by_model: Vec<(String, f64)>,\n}\n\nimpl MetricsPanelData {\n    /// Create new empty metrics panel data.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self::default()\n    }\n\n    /// Set loading state.\n    pub fn loading() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            is_loading: true,\n            ..Default::default()\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set error state.\n    pub fn with_error(error: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            error: Some(error.into()),\n            ..Default::default()\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Update with today's performance data.\n    pub fn set_today(&mut self, today: DailyStat) {\n        self.today = Some(today);\n        self.last_update = Some(Utc::now());\n        self.is_loading = false;\n    }\n\n    /// Update with hourly statistics.\n    pub fn set_hourly_stats(&mut self, stats: Vec<HourlyStat>) {\n        self.hourly_stats = stats;\n    }\n\n    /// Update with model performance data.\n    pub fn set_model_performance(&mut self, models: Vec<ModelPerformance>) {\n        self.model_performance = models;\n    }\n\n    /// Update with worker efficiency data.\n    pub fn set_worker_efficiency(&mut self, workers: Vec<WorkerEfficiency>) {\n        self.worker_efficiency = workers;\n    }\n\n    /// Set real-time metrics from log parsing.\n    pub fn set_realtime(&mut self, realtime: RealtimeMetrics) {\n        self.realtime = realtime;\n    }\n\n    /// Set 7-day task trend data.\n    pub fn set_task_trend_7day(&mut self, trend: Vec<i64>) {\n        self.task_trend_7day = trend;\n    }\n\n    /// Set 7-day cost trend data.\n    pub fn set_cost_trend_7day(&mut self, trend: Vec<f64>) {\n        self.cost_trend_7day = trend;\n    }\n\n    /// Set tasks per hour data.\n    pub fn set_tasks_per_hour(&mut self, data: Vec<i64>) {\n        self.tasks_per_hour = data;\n    }\n\n    /// Set model performance for 7 days.\n    pub fn set_model_performance_7day(&mut self, models: Vec<ModelPerformance>) {\n        self.model_performance_7day = models;\n    }\n\n    /// Set worker efficiency for 7 days.\n    pub fn set_worker_efficiency_7day(&mut self, workers: Vec<WorkerEfficiency>) {\n        self.worker_efficiency_7day = workers;\n    }\n\n    /// Set average cost by model.\n    pub fn set_avg_cost_by_model(&mut self, data: Vec<(String, f64)>) {\n        self.avg_cost_by_model = data;\n    }\n\n    /// Get today's completed tasks.\n    pub fn today_completed(&self) -> i64 {\n        self.today.as_ref().map(|t| t.tasks_completed).unwrap_or(0)\n    }\n\n    /// Get today's failed tasks.\n    pub fn today_failed(&self) -> i64 {\n        self.today.as_ref().map(|t| t.tasks_failed).unwrap_or(0)\n    }\n\n    /// Get today's total tasks.\n    pub fn today_total(&self) -> i64 {\n        self.today_completed() + self.today_failed()\n    }\n\n    /// Get today's success rate.\n    pub fn today_success_rate(&self) -> f64 {\n        self.today\n            .as_ref()\n            .map(|t| t.success_rate * 100.0)\n            .unwrap_or(100.0)\n    }\n\n    /// Get today's average cost per task.\n    pub fn today_avg_cost(&self) -> f64 {\n        self.today\n            .as_ref()\n            .map(|t| t.avg_cost_per_task)\n            .unwrap_or(0.0)\n    }\n\n    /// Get tasks per hour for today.\n    pub fn tasks_per_hour(&self) -> f64 {\n        self.today\n            .as_ref()\n            .map(|t| {\n                let total_tasks = t.tasks_completed + t.tasks_failed;\n                if total_tasks == 0 {\n                    0.0\n                } else {\n                    // Assume 24 hours for daily stats\n                    total_tasks as f64 / 24.0\n                }\n            })\n            .unwrap_or(0.0)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if we have any data.\n    pub fn has_data(&self) -> bool {\n        self.today.is_some()\n            || !self.hourly_stats.is_empty()\n            || !self.model_performance.is_empty()\n            || !self.worker_efficiency.is_empty()\n            || self.realtime.has_data()\n            || !self.task_trend_7day.is_empty()\n            || !self.cost_trend_7day.is_empty()\n            || !self.tasks_per_hour.is_empty()\n            || !self.model_performance_7day.is_empty()\n            || !self.worker_efficiency_7day.is_empty()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Renders a sparkline from a series of values.\npub fn render_sparkline(values: &[i64], width: usize) -> String {\n    if values.is_empty() {\n        return \" \".repeat(width);\n    }\n\n    let blocks = ['▁', '▂', '▃', '▄', '▅', '▆', '▇', '█'];\n    let max = values.iter().cloned().fold(0i64, i64::max);\n    let min = values.iter().cloned().fold(i64::MAX, i64::min);\n    let range = (max - min) as f64;\n\n    // Sample values to fit width\n    let step = values.len() as f64 / width as f64;\n    let mut result = String::with_capacity(width);\n\n    for i in 0..width {\n        let idx = ((i as f64) * step).min(values.len() as f64 - 1.0) as usize;\n        let val = values[idx];\n\n        let normalized = if range > 0.0 {\n            ((val - min) as f64 / range).clamp(0.0, 1.0)\n        } else {\n            0.5\n        };\n\n        let block_idx = ((normalized * 7.0).round() as usize).min(7);\n        result.push(blocks[block_idx]);\n    }\n\n    result\n}\n\n/// Renders a horizontal bar chart.\npub fn render_bar(\n    value: f64,\n    max: f64,\n    width: usize,\n    filled_char: char,\n    empty_char: char,\n) -> String {\n    let pct = if max > 0.0 {\n        (value / max).clamp(0.0, 1.0)\n    } else {\n        0.0\n    };\n    let filled = (pct * width as f64).round() as usize;\n    let empty = width.saturating_sub(filled);\n\n    format!(\n        \"{}{}\",\n        filled_char.to_string().repeat(filled),\n        empty_char.to_string().repeat(empty)\n    )\n}\n\n/// Format a duration in seconds to human-readable form.\npub fn format_duration(secs: i64) -> String {\n    if secs < 60 {\n        format!(\"{}s\", secs)\n    } else if secs < 3600 {\n        let mins = secs / 60;\n        let remaining_secs = secs % 60;\n        format!(\"{}m {}s\", mins, remaining_secs)\n    } else {\n        let hours = secs / 3600;\n        let mins = (secs % 3600) / 60;\n        format!(\"{}h {}m\", hours, mins)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Format a dollar amount for display.\npub fn format_usd(amount: f64) -> String {\n    if amount >= 1000.0 {\n        format!(\"${:.2}K\", amount / 1000.0)\n    } else if amount >= 100.0 {\n        format!(\"${:.1}\", amount)\n    } else if amount >= 10.0 {\n        format!(\"${:.2}\", amount)\n    } else if amount >= 1.0 {\n        format!(\"${:.3}\", amount)\n    } else if amount > 0.0 {\n        format!(\"${:.4}\", amount)\n    } else {\n        \"$0.00\".to_string()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Truncate model name for display.\npub fn truncate_model_name(model: &str, max_len: usize) -> String {\n    // Common abbreviations\n    let name = model\n        .replace(\"claude-opus-4-5-20251101\", \"Opus-4.5\")\n        .replace(\"claude-opus-4-6\", \"Opus-4.6\")\n        .replace(\"claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929\", \"Sonnet-4.5\")\n        .replace(\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\", \"Haiku-4.5\")\n        .replace(\"glm-4.7\", \"GLM-4.7\")\n        .replace(\"deepseek-\", \"DS-\");\n\n    if name.len() <= max_len {\n        name\n    } else if max_len > 2 {\n        format!(\"{}…\", &name[..max_len - 1])\n    } else {\n        name[..max_len].to_string()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Performance metrics panel widget.\npub struct MetricsPanel<'a> {\n    data: &'a MetricsPanelData,\n    focused: bool,\n}\n\nimpl<'a> MetricsPanel<'a> {\n    /// Create a new metrics panel.\n    pub fn new(data: &'a MetricsPanelData) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            data,\n            focused: false,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set focus state.\n    pub fn focused(mut self, focused: bool) -> Self {\n        self.focused = focused;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Render the summary section.\n    fn render_summary(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {\n        let mut lines = Vec::new();\n\n        // Main stats row\n        let completed = self.data.today_completed();\n        let failed = self.data.today_failed();\n        let success_rate = self.data.today_success_rate();\n        let total = self.data.today_total();\n\n        let success_color = if success_rate >= 90.0 {\n            Color::Green\n        } else if success_rate >= 70.0 {\n            Color::Yellow\n        } else {\n            Color::Red\n        };\n\n        lines.push(Line::from(vec![\n            Span::raw(\"Tasks: \"),\n            Span::styled(\n                format!(\"{}\", completed),\n                Style::default()\n                    .fg(Color::Green)\n                    .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),\n            ),\n            Span::raw(\" done, \"),\n            Span::styled(format!(\"{}\", failed), Style::default().fg(Color::Red)),\n            Span::raw(\" failed of \"),\n            Span::styled(format!(\"{}\", total), Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),\n            Span::raw(\" total\"),\n        ]));\n\n        lines.push(Line::from(vec![\n            Span::raw(\"Success: \"),\n            Span::styled(\n                format!(\"{:.1}%\", success_rate),\n                Style::default()\n                    .fg(success_color)\n                    .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),\n            ),\n            Span::raw(\"  \"),\n            Span::raw(\"Avg Cost: \"),\n            Span::styled(\n                format_usd(self.data.today_avg_cost()),\n                Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan),\n            ),\n            Span::raw(\"/task\"),\n        ]));\n\n        // Tasks per hour\n        let tph = self.data.tasks_per_hour();\n        lines.push(Line::from(vec![\n            Span::raw(\"Throughput: \"),\n            Span::styled(\n                format!(\"{:.1} tasks/hr\", tph),\n                Style::default()\n                    .fg(Color::Magenta)\n                    .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),\n            ),\n        ]));\n\n        // Real-time API call metrics from log parsing\n        if self.data.realtime.has_data() {\n            lines.push(Line::from(\"\"));\n            lines.push(Line::from(vec![\n                Span::styled(\"⚡ Live\", Style::default().fg(Color::Magenta).add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD)),\n                Span::raw(\" \"),\n                Span::styled(\n                    format!(\"{} API calls\", self.data.realtime.total_calls),\n                    Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan),\n                ),\n            ]));\n            lines.push(Line::from(vec![\n                Span::raw(\"   Tokens: \"),\n                Span::styled(\n                    format!(\"{}\", self.data.realtime.total_tokens()),\n                    Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan),\n                ),\n                Span::raw(\"  Cost: \"),\n                Span::styled(\n                    format_usd(self.data.realtime.total_cost),\n                    Style::default().fg(Color::Green),\n                ),\n            ]));\n        }\n\n        let paragraph = Paragraph::new(lines);\n        paragraph.render(area, buf);\n    }\n\n    /// Render the tasks per hour histogram.\n    fn render_histogram(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {\n        if self.data.hourly_stats.is_empty() {\n            let msg = Paragraph::new(\"No hourly data\").style(Style::default().fg(Color::Gray));\n            msg.render(area, buf);\n            return;\n        }\n\n        let mut lines = Vec::new();\n        lines.push(Line::from(vec![\n            Span::styled(\n                \"Tasks/Hour\",\n                Style::default()\n                    .fg(Color::Cyan)\n                    .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),\n            ),\n            Span::raw(\" (last 24h)\"),\n        ]));\n        lines.push(Line::from(\"\"));\n\n        // Get tasks completed per hour\n        let values: Vec<i64> = self\n            .data\n            .hourly_stats\n            .iter()\n            .map(|h| h.tasks_completed)\n            .collect();\n\n        let max_val = values.iter().cloned().fold(0i64, i64::max);\n\n        // Render horizontal histogram with bars\n        for (i, &val) in values.iter().enumerate() {\n            // Only show every 4th hour label to save space\n            let show_label = i % 4 == 0;\n            let hour_label = if show_label {\n                format!(\"{:>2}h\", i)\n            } else {\n                \"   \".to_string()\n            };\n\n            let bar_width = (area.width.saturating_sub(8) as usize).max(10);\n            let bar = render_bar(val as f64, max_val as f64, bar_width, '█', '░');\n\n            let bar_color = if val == 0 {\n                Color::DarkGray\n            } else if val >= (max_val / 2) {\n                Color::Green\n            } else {\n                Color::Cyan\n            };\n\n            lines.push(Line::from(vec![\n                Span::raw(hour_label),\n                Span::raw(\" \"),\n                Span::styled(bar, Style::default().fg(bar_color)),\n                Span::styled(format!(\" {:>3}\", val), Style::default().fg(Color::Gray)),\n            ]));\n        }\n\n        let paragraph = Paragraph::new(lines);\n        paragraph.render(area, buf);\n    }\n\n    /// Render the model efficiency comparison table with 5+ metrics.\n    fn render_model_comparison(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {\n        // Use 7-day aggregated data if available\n        let models = if !self.data.model_performance_7day.is_empty() {\n            &self.data.model_performance_7day\n        } else {\n            &self.data.model_performance\n        };\n\n        if models.is_empty() {\n            let msg = Paragraph::new(\"No model data\").style(Style::default().fg(Color::Gray));\n            msg.render(area, buf);\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Sort by total cost (descending) to show most expensive models first\n        let mut sorted_models = models.clone();\n        sorted_models.sort_by(|a, b| b.total_cost_usd.partial_cmp(&a.total_cost_usd).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));\n\n        let rows: Vec<Row> = sorted_models\n            .iter()\n            .take(6)\n            .map(|m| {\n                let success_pct = (m.success_rate * 100.0) as i64;\n\n                // Format cache hit rate\n                let cache_pct = (m.cache_hit_rate * 100.0) as i64;\n\n                Row::new(vec![\n                    truncate_model_name(&m.model, 10),\n                    format!(\"{}\", m.total_calls),\n                    format!(\"{}\", m.tasks_completed),\n                    format!(\"{:.0}%\", success_pct),\n                    format_usd(m.avg_cost_per_task),\n                    format!(\"{:.0}%\", cache_pct),\n                ])\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        let header = Row::new(vec![\"Model\", \"Calls\", \"Done\", \"Success\", \"Cost/T\", \"Cache\"])\n            .style(\n                Style::default()\n                    .fg(Color::Gray)\n                    .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),\n            )\n            .bottom_margin(0);\n\n        let widths = [\n            Constraint::Length(10),\n            Constraint::Length(5),\n            Constraint::Length(4),\n            Constraint::Length(6),\n            Constraint::Length(7),\n            Constraint::Length(5),\n        ];\n\n        let table = Table::new(rows, widths).header(header).column_spacing(1);\n\n        ratatui::widgets::Widget::render(table, area, buf);\n    }\n\n    /// Render the trend sparkline.\n    fn render_trend(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {\n        if self.data.hourly_stats.is_empty() {\n            let msg = Paragraph::new(\"No trend data\").style(Style::default().fg(Color::Gray));\n            msg.render(area, buf);\n            return;\n        }\n\n        let values: Vec<i64> = self\n            .data\n            .hourly_stats\n            .iter()\n            .map(|h| h.tasks_completed)\n            .collect();\n\n        let sparkline = render_sparkline(&values, area.width.saturating_sub(10) as usize);\n\n        let mut lines = Vec::new();\n        lines.push(Line::from(vec![\n            Span::styled(\"Task Trend\", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),\n            Span::raw(\" (24h)\"),\n        ]));\n        lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(\n            sparkline,\n            Style::default().fg(Color::Green),\n        )));\n\n        let min_val = values.iter().cloned().fold(i64::MAX, i64::min);\n        let max_val = values.iter().cloned().fold(0i64, i64::max);\n        lines.push(Line::from(vec![\n            Span::styled(format!(\"{}\", min_val), Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),\n            Span::raw(\" - \"),\n            Span::styled(format!(\"{}\", max_val), Style::default().fg(Color::White)),\n        ]));\n\n        let paragraph = Paragraph::new(lines);\n        paragraph.render(area, buf);\n    }\n\n    /// Render the 7-day trend sparkline.\n    fn render_7day_trend(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {\n        // Use the new 7-day trend data if available, otherwise fall back to hourly\n        let values = if !self.data.task_trend_7day.is_empty() {\n            self.data.task_trend_7day.clone()\n        } else {\n            self.data\n                .hourly_stats\n                .iter()\n                .map(|h| h.tasks_completed)\n                .collect()\n        };\n\n        if values.is_empty() {\n            let msg = Paragraph::new(\"No 7-day trend data\").style(Style::default().fg(Color::Gray));\n            msg.render(area, buf);\n            return;\n        }\n\n        let sparkline = render_sparkline(&values, area.width.saturating_sub(14) as usize);\n\n        let total: i64 = values.iter().sum();\n        let avg = if !values.is_empty() {\n            total as f64 / values.len() as f64\n        } else {\n            0.0\n        };\n\n        let mut lines = Vec::new();\n        lines.push(Line::from(vec![\n            Span::styled(\"7-Day Trend\", Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),\n            Span::raw(\"  \"),\n            Span::styled(\n                format!(\"Total: {}\", total),\n                Style::default().fg(Color::White),\n            ),\n            Span::raw(\"  \"),\n            Span::styled(\n                format!(\"Avg: {:.1}/day\", avg),\n                Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray),\n            ),\n        ]));\n        lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(\n            sparkline,\n            Style::default().fg(Color::Green),\n        )));\n\n        let paragraph = Paragraph::new(lines);\n        paragraph.render(area, buf);\n    }\n\n    /// Render the worker efficiency ranking.\n    fn render_worker_efficiency(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {\n        let workers = if !self.data.worker_efficiency_7day.is_empty() {\n            &self.data.worker_efficiency_7day\n        } else {\n            &self.data.worker_efficiency\n        };\n\n        if workers.is_empty() {\n            let msg = Paragraph::new(\"No worker data\").style(Style::default().fg(Color::Gray));\n            msg.render(area, buf);\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Sort by tasks completed (descending)\n        let mut sorted_workers = workers.clone();\n        sorted_workers.sort_by_key(|b| std::cmp::Reverse(b.tasks_completed));\n\n        let mut lines = Vec::new();\n        lines.push(Line::from(vec![\n            Span::styled(\n                \"Worker Efficiency\",\n                Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan).add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),\n            ),\n            Span::raw(\" (7-day)\"),\n        ]));\n        lines.push(Line::from(\"\"));\n\n        // Show top 6 workers\n        for worker in sorted_workers.iter().take(6) {\n            let success_color = if worker.success_rate >= 0.9 {\n                Color::Green\n            } else if worker.success_rate >= 0.7 {\n                Color::Yellow\n            } else {\n                Color::Red\n            };\n\n            // Calculate tasks per day (7-day period)\n            let tasks_per_day = worker.tasks_completed as f64 / 7.0;\n\n            let worker_name = if worker.worker_id.len() > 18 {\n                format!(\"{}…\", &worker.worker_id[..15])\n            } else {\n                worker.worker_id.clone()\n            };\n\n            lines.push(Line::from(vec![\n                Span::styled(\n                    format!(\"{:<18}\", worker_name),\n                    Style::default().fg(Color::White),\n                ),\n                Span::raw(\" \"),\n                Span::styled(\n                    format!(\"{:>3}\", worker.tasks_completed),\n                    Style::default().fg(Color::Green),\n                ),\n                Span::raw(\" tasks  \"),\n                Span::styled(\n                    format!(\"{:>4.1}/d\", tasks_per_day),\n                    Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan),\n                ),\n                Span::raw(\"  \"),\n                Span::styled(\n                    format!(\"{:>5.1}%\", worker.success_rate * 100.0),\n                    Style::default().fg(success_color),\n                ),\n                Span::raw(\"  \"),\n                Span::styled(\n                    format_usd(worker.avg_cost_per_task),\n                    Style::default().fg(Color::Magenta),\n                ),\n            ]));\n        }\n\n        let paragraph = Paragraph::new(lines);\n        paragraph.render(area, buf);\n    }\n\n}\n\nimpl Widget for MetricsPanel<'_> {\n    fn render(self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {\n        // Enhanced focus indicator: \"▶\" arrow for focused (clearer than diamond), \"▪\" for unfocused\n        let focus_icon = if self.focused { \"▶\" } else { \"▪\" };\n\n        // Border type: Double border for focused (highly visible), Normal for unfocused\n        let border_type = if self.focused {\n            BorderType::Double\n        } else {\n            BorderType::Plain\n        };\n\n        // Border style: bright cyan with bold for focused, dim for unfocused\n        let border_style = if self.focused {\n            Style::default()\n                .fg(Color::Cyan)\n                .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD)\n        } else {\n            Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)\n        };\n\n        // Title style: bold + underlined for focused, dim for unfocused\n        let title_style = if self.focused {\n            Style::default()\n                .fg(Color::Cyan)\n                .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD | Modifier::UNDERLINED)\n        } else {\n            Style::default().fg(Color::Rgb(80, 80, 80))\n        };\n\n        let block = Block::default()\n            .borders(Borders::ALL)\n            .border_type(border_type)\n            .border_style(border_style)\n            .title(Span::styled(\n                format!(\" {} Performance Analytics \", focus_icon),\n                title_style,\n            ));\n\n        let inner = block.inner(area);\n        block.render(area, buf);\n\n        // Handle special states\n        if self.data.is_loading {\n            let loading =\n                Paragraph::new(\"Loading metrics...\").style(Style::default().fg(Color::Yellow));\n            loading.render(inner, buf);\n            return;\n        }\n\n        if let Some(ref error) = self.data.error {\n            let err =\n                Paragraph::new(format!(\"Error: {}\", error)).style(Style::default().fg(Color::Red));\n            err.render(inner, buf);\n            return;\n        }\n\n        if !self.data.has_data() {\n            let no_data = Paragraph::new(\n                \"No performance data available.\\n\\n\\\n                 Metrics tracking requires:\\n\\\n                 - forge-cost database initialized\\n\\\n                 - Worker activity being logged\\n\\n\\\n                 Run: forge costs init\",\n            )\n            .style(Style::default().fg(Color::Gray));\n            no_data.render(inner, buf);\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Determine layout based on available height\n        // For wider displays, use a 2-column layout\n        let is_wide = area.width >= 160;\n\n        if is_wide {\n            self.render_wide_layout(inner, buf);\n        } else {\n            self.render_narrow_layout(inner, buf);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl MetricsPanel<'_> {\n    /// Render wide layout (2 columns) for screens >= 160 columns\n    fn render_wide_layout(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {\n        // Split into left and right columns\n        let columns = Layout::default()\n            .direction(Direction::Horizontal)\n            .constraints([Constraint::Percentage(50), Constraint::Percentage(50)])\n            .split(area);\n\n        // Left column: Summary + Histogram + 7-day Trend\n        let left_layout = Layout::default()\n            .direction(Direction::Vertical)\n            .constraints([\n                Constraint::Length(4),  // Summary\n                Constraint::Min(10),    // Histogram (24h)\n                Constraint::Length(4),  // 7-day Trend\n            ])\n            .split(columns[0]);\n\n        // Right column: Model Comparison + Worker Efficiency\n        let right_layout = Layout::default()\n            .direction(Direction::Vertical)\n            .constraints([\n                Constraint::Min(8),  // Model comparison\n                Constraint::Min(6),  // Worker efficiency\n            ])\n            .split(columns[1]);\n\n        self.render_summary(left_layout[0], buf);\n        self.render_histogram(left_layout[1], buf);\n        self.render_7day_trend(left_layout[2], buf);\n        self.render_model_comparison(right_layout[0], buf);\n        self.render_worker_efficiency(right_layout[1], buf);\n    }\n\n    /// Render narrow layout (single column) for screens < 160 columns\n    fn render_narrow_layout(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {\n        let layout = Layout::default()\n            .direction(Direction::Vertical)\n            .constraints([\n                Constraint::Length(3), // Summary\n                Constraint::Min(6),    // Histogram\n                Constraint::Min(5),    // Model comparison\n                Constraint::Length(4), // 7-day Trend\n                Constraint::Min(4),    // Worker efficiency\n            ])\n            .split(area);\n\n        self.render_summary(layout[0], buf);\n        self.render_histogram(layout[1], buf);\n        self.render_model_comparison(layout[2], buf);\n        self.render_trend(layout[3], buf);\n        self.render_worker_efficiency(layout[4], buf);\n    }\n}\n\n/// Compact metrics summary widget for overview panel.\npub struct MetricsSummaryCompact<'a> {\n    data: &'a success_color = if success_rate >= 90.0 {\n            Color::Green\n        } else if success_rate >= 70.0 {\n            Color::Yellow\n        } else {\n            Color::Red\n        };\n\n \"),\n            Span::styled(\n                format!(\"{}\", completed),\n                Style::default()\n                    .fg(Color::Green)\n                    .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),\n            ),\n        ]));\n\n        // Failed tasks\n        if failed > 0 {\n            lines.push(Line::from(vec![\n                Span::styled(\"✗\", .get(\"args\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_sequence())\n            .map(|seq| {\n                seq.iter()\n                    .filter_map(|v| v.as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n                    .collect()\n            })\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n\n        // Read model (optional, defaults to \"sonnet\")\n        let model = chat_backend\n            .get(\"model\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .unwrap_or(\"sonnet\")\n            .to_string();\n\n        // Determine provider: explicit `provider` field takes precedence; fall back to\n        // inferring from `command` for backwards compatibility (opencode → opencode,\n        // anything else → claude-cli).\n        let provider_name = chat_backend\n            .get(\"provider\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| {\n                if command == \"opencode\" {\n                    \"opencode\"\n                } else {\n                    \"claude-cli\"\n                }\n            });\n\n        info!(\"⏱️ Using provider: {}\", provider_name);\n\n        // Warn about command/provider field mismatches before delegating to factory.\n        match provider_name {\n            \"opencode\" if command != \"opencode\" => {\n                warn!(\n                    \"Provider is 'opencode' but command is '{}' — using command as binary path\",\n                    command\n                );\n            }\n            \"claude-cli\" if command == \"opencode\" => {\n                warn!(\n                    \"Provider is 'claude-cli' but command is 'opencode' — this may not work as \\\n                     expected. Set provider: opencode to use the OpenCode provider.\"\n                );\n            }\n            _ => {}\n        }\n\n        // Read model_aliases (only consumed by the opencode provider).\n        let model_aliases = chat_backend\n            .get(\"model_aliases\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_mapping())\n            .map(|m| {\n                m.iter()\n                    .filter_map(|(k, v)| {\n                        let key = k.as_str()?.to_string();\n                        let val = v.as_str()?.to_string();\n                        Some((key, val))\n                    })\n                    .collect::<std::collections::HashMap<String, String>>()\n            });\n\n        // Use the factory to build the ProviderConfig from the provider name string.\n        let provider_config = match ChatProviderFactory::create_config(\n            provider_name,\n            command,\n            &model,\n            args,\n            model_aliases,\n        ) {\n            Ok(config) => config,\n            Err(e) => {\n                error!(\"Failed to create provider config: {}\", e);\n                return None;\n            }\n        };\n\n        let chat_config = ChatConfig {\n            provider: provider_config,\n            rate_limit: RateLimitConfig::default(),\n            audit: AuditConfig::default(),\n            confirmations: ConfirmationConfig::default(),\n        };\n\n        // Initialize backend (async, but we block here during startup)\n        let runtime_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n        match tokio::runtime::Runtime::new() {\n            Ok(rt) => {\n                info!(\n                    \"⏱️ Created tokio runtime for chat backend in {:?}\",\n                    runtime_start.elapsed()\n                );\n                let backend_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n                match rt.block_on(ChatBackend::new(chat_config)) {\n                    Ok(backend) => {\n                        info!(\n                            \"⏱️ ✅ Chat backend initialized successfully in {:?} (total: {:?})\",\n                            backend_start.elapsed(),\n                            start.elapsed()\n                        );\n                        Some(backend)\n                    }\n                    Err(e) => {\n                        error!(\n                            \"⏱️ ❌ Failed to initialize chat backend: {} (took {:?})\",\n                            e,\n                            start.elapsed()\n                        );\n                        None\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                error!(\n                    \"⏱️ ❌ Failed to create tokio runtime: {} (took {:?})\",\n                    e,\n                    start.elapsed()\n                );\n                None\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Initialize chat backend from config.yaml with worker spawner support.\n    ///\n    /// Returns None if config is missing or initialization fails.","numLines":200,"startLine":700,"totalLines":7486}}}

ConfirmationConfig, RateLimitConfig};\n        use forge_chat::ChatProviderFactory;\n        use tracing::{error, info, warn};\n\n        let start = std::time::Instant::now();\n        info!(\"⏱️ init_chat_backend() started\");\n\n        // Load config from ~/.forge/config.yaml\n        let config_path = match dirs::home_dir() {\n            Some(home) => home.join(\".forge/config.yaml\"),\n (took {:?})\",\n                config_path.display(),\n                start.elapsed()\n            );\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        let read_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n provider: explicit `provider` field takes precedence; fall back to\n        // inferring from `command` for backwards compatibility (opencode → opencode,\n        // anything else → claude-cli).\n    /// Initialize chat backend from config.yaml.\n    ///\n    /// Returns None if config is missing or initialization fails.\n    /// Errors are logged but don't prevent app startup.\n    fn init_chat_backend() -> Option<ChatBackend> {\n        use forge_chat::config::{AuditConfig, ConfirmationConfig, RateLimitConfig};\n        use forge_chat::ChatProviderFactory;\n        use tracing::{error, info, warn};\n\n        let start = std::time::Instant::now();\n        info!(\"⏱️ init_chat_backend() started\");\n\n        // Load config from ~/.forge/config.yaml\n        let config_path = match dirs::home_dir() {\n            Some(home) => home.join(\".forge/config.yaml\"),\n            None => {\n                warn!(\"⏱️ Could not determine home directory (took {:?})\", start.elapsed());\n                return None;\n            }\n        };\n\n        info!(\n            \"⏱️ Initializing chat backend from {}\",\n            config_path.display()\n        );\n\n        if !config_path.exists() {\n            warn!(\n                \"⏱️ Chat config not found at {} (took {:?})\",\n                config_path.display(),\n                start.elapsed()\n            );\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        let read_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n        let config_str = match std::fs::read_to_string(&config_path) {\n            Ok(s) => s,\n            Err(e) => {\n                error!(\n                    \"⏱️ Failed to read chat config: {} (took {:?})\",\n                    e,\n                    start.elapsed()\n                );\n                return None;\n            }\n        };\n        info!(\"⏱️ Config file read in {:?}\", read_start.elapsed());\n\n        // Parse the full config YAML\n        let parse_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n        let yaml: serde_yaml::Value = match serde_yaml::from_str(&config_str) {\n            Ok(v) => v,\n            Err(e) => {\n                warn!(\n                    \"⏱️ Failed to parse config YAML: {} (took {:?})\",\n                    e,\n                    start.elapsed()\n                );\n                return None;\n            }\n        };\n chat_backend.get(\"command\")?.as_str()?;\n\n        // Read optional args (not required — providers may handle args internally)\n        let args: Vec<String> = chat_backend\n            .get(\"args\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_sequence())\n            .map(|seq| {\n                seq.iter()\n                    .filter_map(|v| v.as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n   .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .unwrap_or(\"sonnet\")\n            .to_string();\n\n        // Determine provider: explicit `provider` field takes precedence; fall back to\n        // inferring from `command` for backwards compatibility (opencode → opencode,\n        // anything else → claude-cli).\n        let provider_name = chat_backend\n            .get(\"provider\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| {\n                if command == \"opencode\" {\n                    \"opencode\"\n                } else {\n                    \"claude-cli\"\n                }\n            });\n\n        info!(\"⏱️ Using provider: {}\", provider_name);\n\n        // Warn about command/provider field mismatches before delegating to factory.\n        match provider_name {\n            \"opencode\" if command != \"opencode\" => {\n                warn!(\n                    \"Provider is 'opencode' but command is '{}' — using command as binary path\",\n                    command\n                );\n            }\n            \"claude-cli\" if command == \"opencode\" => {\n                warn!(\n                    \"Provider is 'claude-cli' but command is 'opencode' — this may not work as \\\n                     expected. Set provider: opencode to use the OpenCode provider.\"\n                );\n            }\n            _ => {}\n        }\n\n        // Read model_aliases (only consumed by the opencode provider).\n .filter_map(|(k, v)| {\n                        let key = k.as_str()?.to_string();\n                        let val = v.as_str()?.to_string();\n                        Some((key, val))\n                    })\n                    .collect::<std::collections::HashMap<String, String>>()\n            });\n\n        // Use the factory to build the ProviderConfig from the provider name string.\n        Ok(config) => config,\n            Err(e) => {\n                error!(\"Failed to create provider config: {}\", e);\n                return None;\n            }\n        };\n\n        let Main application state and logic for the FORGE TUI.\n//!\n//! The `App` struct manages overall application state, view switching,\n//! and coordinates between different components.\n//!\n//! # Chat Backend Integration Architecture\n//!\n//! The chat functionality integrates an async `ChatBackend` into the synchronous\n//! TUI event loop using the following design:\n//!\n//! ## Async-to-Sync Bridge\n//!\n//! 1. **Tokio Runtime**: A single-threaded tokio runtime is created during App::new()\n//!    and lives for the duration of the application. This runtime handles all async\n//!    chat operations.\n//!\n//! 2. **Message Passing**: An `mpsc` channel connects async chat tasks to the sync UI:\n//!    - `chat_tx`: Sender held by App, used to submit chat requests\n//!    - `chat_rx`: Receiver held by a background task, processes requests\n//!    - `response_rx`: Receiver held by App, receives responses\n//!\n//! 3. **Non-Blocking Execution**: When user submits chat input:\n//!    - Send request to background task via `chat_tx`\n//!    - Set `chat_pending` flag and display loading indicator\n//!    - Continue UI event loop without blocking\n//!    - Poll `response_rx` each frame to check for responses\n//!\n//! ## State Management\n//!\n//! - `chat_pending: bool` - Whether a request is in flight\n//! - `chat_history: Vec<ChatExchange>` - Last 10 exchanges (user query + response)\n//! - `chat_backend: Option<ChatBackend>` - Initialized from config.yaml\n//!\n//! ## Error Handling\n//!\n//! - Backend initialization failures: App runs without chat (display error in UI)\n//! - Request timeout: Background task implements 30s timeout\n//! - API errors: Captured in response, displayed in chat history as error messages\n//!\n//! ## Performance\n//!\n//! - Background task prevents UI blocking\n//! - Response polling adds <1ms per frame\n//! - Chat history limited to 10 exchanges (~1KB memory)\n\nuse bool,\n    /// Input buffer for config editing\n    config_menu_input: String,\n}\n\n/// Temporary storage for chat exchange data during streaming display.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug)]\nstruct PendingChatExchange {\n    query: String,\n    response_text: String,\n    timestamp: String,\n    tool_calls: Vec<ToolCallInfo>,\n    side_effects: Vec<SideEffectInfo>,\n    confirmation: Option<ConfirmationInfo>,\n    metadata: ResponseMetadata,\n}\n\n/// A single chat exchange (user query + assistant response).\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]\npub struct ChatExchange {\n    pub user_query: String,\n    pub assistant_response: String,\n    pub timestamp: String,\n    pub is_error: bool,\n    /// Tool calls made during this exchange\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub tool_calls: Vec<ToolCallInfo>,\n    /// Side effects from tool execution\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub side_effects: Vec<SideEffectInfo>,\n    /// Confirmation prompt if action requires approval\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub confirmation: Option<ConfirmationInfo>,\n    /// Response metadata (duration, cost, provider)\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub metadata: ResponseMetadata,\n    /// Actionable guidance for errors (how to fix)\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub error_guidance: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Information about a tool call for display.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]\npub struct ToolCallInfo {\n    /// Tool name (e.g., \"spawn_worker\", \"get_worker_status\")\n    pub name: String,\n    /// Whether the tool execution succeeded\n    pub success: bool,\n    /// Human-readable result message\n    pub message: String,\n}\n\n/// Information about a side effect for display.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]\npub struct SideEffectInfo {\n    /// Type of effect (e.g., \"spawn\", \"kill\", \"assign\")\n    pub effect_type: String,\n    /// Description of what happened\n    pub description: String,\n}\n\n/// Confirmation prompt information for display.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]\npub struct ConfirmationInfo {\n    /// Title of the confirmation\n    pub title: String,\n    /// Description of what will happen\n    pub description: String,\n    /// Warning level (info, warning, danger)\n    pub level: ConfirmationLevel,\n    /// Estimated cost impact\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub cost_impact: Option<f64>,\n    /// Items that will be affected\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub affected_items: Vec<String>,\n    /// Whether this action is reversible\n    pub reversible: bool,\n}\n\n/// Warning level for confirmation prompts.\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]\npub enum ConfirmationLevel {\n    /// Informational (no danger)\n    Info,\n    /// Warning (some risk)\n    Warning,\n    /// Danger (high risk, destructive)\n    Danger,\n}\n\n/// Response metadata for display.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Default, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]\npub struct ResponseMetadata {\n    /// Duration in milliseconds\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub duration_ms: u64,\n    /// Estimated cost in USD\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub cost_usd: Option<f64>,\n    /// Provider name\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub provider: String,\n}\n\n/// Generate actionable guidance for common errors.\nfn get_error_guidance(error_message: &str) -> Option<String> {\n    let error_lower = error_message.to_lowercase();\n\n    // Config not found errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"config\") && (error_lower.contains(\"not found\") || error_lower.contains(\"missing\")) {\n        return Some(\"Run 'forge init' to create a configuration file, or check that ~/.forge/config.yaml exists.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // API/authentication errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"api key\") || error_lower.contains(\"authentication\") || error_lower.contains(\"unauthorized\") {\n        return Some(\"Check your API key in ~/.forge/config.yaml. Ensure the key is valid and not expired.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Rate limit errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"rate limit\") || error_lower.contains(\"too many requests\") {\n        return Some(\"Wait a moment before sending more commands. Rate limits reset automatically.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Network unreachable (no internet)\n    if error_lower.contains(\"network unreachable\") || error_lower.contains(\"no route to host\") {\n        return Some(\"⚠️  Network unreachable. Check your internet connection and try again when online. Last known state is cached.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // DNS resolution failures\n    if error_lower.contains(\"dns\") || error_lower.contains(\"failed to lookup address\") || error_lower.contains(\"name or service not known\") {\n        return Some(\"⚠️  DNS resolution failed. Check DNS settings or try using 8.8.8.8 as your DNS server. Click 'r' to retry.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Connection timeout\n    if error_lower.contains(\"timeout\") {\n        return Some(\"⏱️  Request timed out. The server may be slow or unreachable. Click 'r' to retry or check status.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Generic connection/network errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"connection\") || error_lower.contains(\"network\") {\n        return Some(\"⚠️  Connection failed. Check your internet connection. The API server may be temporarily unavailable. Click 'r' to retry.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Provider errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"provider\") && error_lower.contains(\"not found\") {\n        return Some(\"Check that the provider is installed and available in your PATH. Run 'forge --help' for setup instructions.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Tool execution errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"tool\") && error_lower.contains(\"failed\") {\n        return Some(\"The requested operation could not be completed. Check the tool output above for details.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Worker errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"worker\") && (error_lower.contains(\"spawn\") || error_lower.contains(\"launch\")) {\n        return Some(\"Ensure you have sufficient system resources and the worker binary is available.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Context gathering errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"context\") && error_lower.contains(\"failed\") {\n        return Some(\"The dashboard context could not be loaded. Try restarting the application.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Permission/access errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"permission\") || error_lower.contains(\"access denied\") || error_lower.contains(\"forbidden\") {\n        return Some(\"You don't have permission for this action. Check your configuration and credentials.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Resource not found errors (general)\n    if error_lower.contains(\"not found\") || error_lower.contains(\"does not exist\") {\n        return Some(\"The requested resource was not found. Verify the name and try again.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // JSON parsing errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"json\") || error_lower.contains(\"parse\") || error_lower.contains(\"deserialize\") {\n        return Some(\"There was a problem processing the response. The server may have returned unexpected data.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // IO errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"io error\") || error_lower.contains(\"file\") && error_lower.contains(\"error\") {\n        return Some(\"A file system error occurred. Check file permissions and available disk space.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    None\n}\n\n/// Result of an update operation.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug)]\npub enum UpdateResult {\n    /// Update completed successfully with version info\n    Success {\n        /// Previous version\n        old_version: String,\n        /// New version\n        new_version: String,\n    },\n    /// Update failed with an error message\n    Failed(String),\n    /// Already up to date\n    AlreadyUpToDate,\n}\n\n/// Progress information for update downloads.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Default)]\npub struct UpdateProgress {\n    /// Current status message\n    pub status: String,\n    /// Download progress percentage (0-100)\n    pub percent: u8,\n    /// Whether the update is in progress\n    pub in_progress: bool,\n}\n\nimpl Default for App {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl App {\n    /// Create a new app instance with default state.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        use tracing::info;\n\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        info!(\"⏱️ App::new() started\");\n\n        let now = Instant::now();\n\n        // Time DataManager initialization\n        let dm_start = Instant::now();\n        info!(\"⏱️ Creating DataManager...\");\n        let data_manager = DataManager::new();\n        info!(\"⏱️ DataManager created in {:?}\", dm_start.elapsed());\n\n        // Time ThemeManager initialization\n        let theme_start = Instant::now();\n        info!(\"⏱️ Loading ThemeManager...\");\n        let theme_manager = ThemeManager::load_config();\n        info!(\"⏱️ ThemeManager loaded in {:?}\", theme_start.elapsed());\n\n        // Initialize worker launcher and runtime (needed before chat backend for worker spawning)\n        let worker_launcher = WorkerLauncher::new();\n        let worker_runtime = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n            .enable_all()\n            .build()\n            .expect(\"Failed to create worker runtime\");\n\n        // Time chat backend initialization\n        let chat_start = Instant::now();\n        info!(\"⏱️ Initializing chat backend...\");\n        let chat_backend = Self::init_chat_backend_with_worker_spawner(&worker_runtime, &worker_launcher).map(Arc::new);\n        info!(\"⏱️ Chat backend initialized in {:?}\", chat_start.elapsed());\n\n        // Initialize config watcher for hot-reload (skip in test mode to save inotify instances)\n        let config_start = Instant::now();\n        info!(\"⏱️ Initializing config watcher...\");\n        let (config_watcher, config_rx, forge_config) = if cfg!(test) {\n            info!(\"⏱️ Skipping config watcher in test mode\");\n            (None, None, ForgeConfig::default())\n        } else {\n            match ConfigWatcher::new() {\n                Some((watcher, rx)) => {\n                    let config = watcher.current_config().clone();\n                    info!(\"⏱️ Config watcher initialized in {:?}\", config_start.elapsed());\n                    (Some(watcher), Some(rx), config)\n                }\n                None => {\n                    info!(\"⏱️ Config watcher not initialized (config file not found)\");\n                    (None, None, ForgeConfig::default())\n                }\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Calculate initial data poll interval from config\n        let data_poll_interval = Duration::from_millis(\n            forge_config.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms.min(DEFAULT_DATA_POLL_INTERVAL_MS)\n        );\n\n        // Configure alert notifier from config\n        let mut data_manager = data_manager;\n        data_manager.configure_notifier(\n            forge_config.notifications.bell_on_critical,\n            forge_config.notifications.bell_on_warning,\n            forge_config.notifications.bell_interval_secs,\n            forge_config.notifications.visual_flash_on_critical,\n        );\n\n        // Initialize history manager and load previous history\n        let history_manager = forge_chat::HistoryManager::new().ok();\n\n        info!(\"⏱️ App::new() completed in {:?}\", start.elapsed());\n\n        Self {\n            current_view: View::default(),\n            previous_view: None,\n            focus_panel: FocusPanel::default(),\n            input_handler: InputHandler::new(),\n            should_quit: false,\n            show_help: false,\n            chat_input: String::new(),\n            status_message: None,\n            scroll_offset: 0,\n            data_manager,\n            theme_manager,\n            dirty: true,\n            cached_size: None,\n            last_poll_time: now,\n            cached_timestamp: None,\n            last_timestamp_update: now,\n            cached_layout_mode: None,\n            last_terminal_width: 0,\n            update_available: false,\n            update_in_progress: false,\n            update_progress: UpdateProgress::default(),\n            update_result_rx: None,\n            update_ready_for_restart: false,\n            last_update_check: now,\n            chat_backend,\n            chat_response_tx: None,\n            chat_response_rx: None,\n            chat_pending: false,\n            chat_spinner_frame: 0,\n            streaming_response: String::new(),\n            streaming_position: 0,\n  chat_history: Vec::new(),\n            chat_scroll_offset: 0,\n            worker_launcher,\n            worker_runtime,\n            show_kill_dialog: false,\n            kill_dialog_workers: Vec::new(),\n            kill_dialog_selected: 0,\n            kill_dialog_error: None,\n            priority_filter: None,\n            task_search_query: String::new(),\n            task_search_mode: false,\n            pending_chat_exchange: None,\n            show_task_detail: false,\n            selected_task_index: 0,\n            selected_alert_index: 0,\n            _config_watcher: config_watcher,\n            config_rx,\n            forge_config,\n            data_poll_interval,\n            show_confirmation: false,\n            pending_action: None,\n            paused_workers: std::collections::HashSet::new(),\n            selected_worker_index: 0,\n            error_recovery: SharedErrorRecoveryManager::new(),\n            last_failed_query: None,\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new app with a custom status directory (for testing).\n    #[allow(dead_code)]\n    pub fn with_status_dir(status_dir: std::path::PathBuf) -> Self {\n        let now = InputHandler::new(),\n            should_quit: false,\n            show_help: false,\n            chat_input: String::new(),\n            status_message: None,\n            scroll_offset: 0,\n            data_manager: DataManager::with_status_dir(status_dir),\n            theme_manager: ThemeManager::new(),\n            dirty: true,\n            cached_size: None,\n            last_poll_time: now,\n            cached_timestamp: None,\n            last_timestamp_update: now,\n            cached_layout_mode: None,\n            last_terminal_width: 0,\n            update_available: false,\n            update_in_progress: false,\n            update_progress: UpdateProgress::default(),\n            update_result_rx: None,\n            update_ready_for_restart: false,\n            last_update_check: now,\n            chat_backend: None, // Don't initialize in test mode\n            chat_response_tx: None,\n            chat_response_rx: None,\n            chat_pending: false,\n            streaming_response: String::new(),\n            streaming_position: 0,\n            streaming_active: false,\n            pending_complete_response: None,\n            streaming_chunk_rx: None,\n            real_streaming_active: false,\n            streaming_query: None,\n            chat_history: Vec::new(),\n            chat_scroll_offset: 0,\n            worker_launcher: worker runtime\"),\n            show_kill_dialog: false,\n            kill_dialog_workers: Vec::new(),\n            kill_dialog_selected: 0,\n            kill_dialog_error: None,\n            priority_filter: None,\n            pending_chat_exchange: None,\n            show_task_detail: false,\n            selected_task_index: 0,\n            selected_alert_index: 0,\n Duration::from_millis(DEFAULT_DATA_POLL_INTERVAL_MS),\n            chat_spinner_frame: 0,\n            show_confirmation: false,\n            pending_action: None,\n            task_search_query: String::new(),\n            task_search_mode: false,\n            paused_workers: std::collections::HashSet::new(),\n            selected_worker_index: 0,\n            error_recovery: SharedErrorRecoveryManager::new(),\n            last_failed_query: None,\n            retry_available: false,\n            network_available: true,\n            network_error_message: None,\n            network_unavailable_since: None,\n            history_manager: None, // Don't initialize in test mode\n            show_config_menu: false,\n            config_menu_type: None,\n            config_menu_selected: 0,\n            config_menu_editing: false,\n            config_menu_input: String::new(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Initialize chat backend from config.yaml.\n    ///\n    /// Returns None if config is missing or initialization fails.\n    /// Errors are logged but don't prevent app startup.\n    fn init_chat_backend() -> Option<ChatBackend> {\n        use forge_chat::config::{AuditConfig, ConfirmationConfig, RateLimitConfig};\n        use forge_chat::ChatProviderFactory;\n        use tracing::{error, info, warn};\n\n        let start = std::time::Instant::now();\n        info!(\"⏱️ init_chat_backend() started\");\n\n        // Load config from ~/.forge/config.yaml\n        let config_path = match dirs::home_dir() {\n            Some(home) => home.join(\".forge/config.yaml\"),\n            None => {\n                warn!(\"⏱️ Could not determine home directory (took {:?})\", start.elapsed());\n                return None;\n            }\n        };\n\n        info!(\n            \"⏱️ Initializing chat backend from {}\",\n            config_path.display()\n        );\n\n        if !config_path.exists() {\n            warn!(\n                \"⏱️ Chat config not found at {} (took {:?})\",\n                config_path.display(),\n                start.elapsed()\n            );\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        let read_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n        let config_str = match std::fs::read_to_string(&config_path) {\n            Ok(s) => s,\n            Err(e) => {\n                error!(\n                    \"⏱️ Failed to read chat config: {} (took {:?})\",\n                    e,\n                    start.elapsed()\n                );\n                return None;\n            }\n        };\n        info!(\"⏱️ Config file read in {:?}\", read_start.elapsed());\n\n        // Parse the full config YAML\n        let parse_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n        let yaml: serde_yaml::Value = match serde_yaml::from_str(&config_str) {\n            Ok(v) => v,\n            Err(e) => {\n                warn!(\n                    \"⏱️ Failed to parse config YAML: {} (took {:?})\",\n                    e,\n                    start.elapsed()\n                );\n                return None;\n            }\n        };\n        info!(\"⏱️ YAML parsed in {:?}\", parse_start.elapsed());\n\n        // Extract chat_backend section\n        let chat_backend = yaml.get(\"chat_backend\")?;\n        let command = chat_backend.get(\"command\")?.as_str()?;\n\n        // Read optional args (not required — providers may handle args internally)\n        let args: Vec<String> = chat_backend\n            .get(\"args\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_sequence())\n            .map(|seq| {\n                seq.iter()\n                    .filter_map(|v| v.as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n                    .collect()\n            })\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n\n        // Read model (optional, defaults to \"sonnet\")\n        let model = chat_backend\n            .get(\"model\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .unwrap_or(\"sonnet\")\n            .to_string();\n\n        // Determine provider: explicit `provider` field takes precedence; fall back to\n        // inferring from `command` for backwards compatibility (opencode → opencode,\n        // anything else → claude-cli).\n        let provider_name = chat_backend\n            .get(\"provider\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| {\n                if command == \"opencode\" {\n                    \"opencode\"\n                } else {\n                    \"claude-cli\"\n                }\n            });\n\n        info!(\"⏱️ Using provider: {}\", provider_name);\n\n        // Warn about command/provider field mismatches before delegating to factory.\n        match provider_name {\n            \"opencode\" if command != \"opencode\" => {\n                warn!(\n                    \"Provider is 'opencode' but command is '{}' — using command as binary path\",\n                    command\n                );\n            }\n            \"claude-cli\" if command == \"opencode\" => {\n                warn!(\n                    \"Provider is 'claude-cli' but command is 'opencode' — this may not work as \\\n                     expected. Set provider: opencode to use the OpenCode provider.\"\n                );\n            }\n            _ => {}\n        }\n\n        // Read model_aliases (only consumed by the opencode provider).\n        let model_aliases = chat_backend\n            .get(\"model_aliases\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_mapping())\n            .map(|m| {\n                m.iter()\n                    .filter_map(|(k, v)| {\n                        let key = k.as_str()?.to_string();\n                        let val = v.as_str()?.to_string();\n                        Some((key, val))\n                    })\n                    .collect::<std::collections::HashMap<String, String>>()\n            });\n\n        // Use the factory to build the ProviderConfig from the provider name string.\n        let provider_config = match ChatProviderFactory::create_config(\n            provider_name,\n            command,\n            &model,\n            args,\n            model_aliases,\n        ) {\n            Ok(config) => config,\n            Err(e) => {\n                error!(\"Failed to create provider config: {}\", e);\n                return None;\n            }\n        };\n\n        let chat_config = ChatConfig {\n            provider: provider_config,\n            rate_limit: RateLimitConfig::default(),\n            audit: AuditConfig::default(),\n            confirmations: ConfirmationConfig::default(),\n        };\n\n        // Initialize backend (async, but we block here during startup)\n        let runtime_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n        match tokio::runtime::Runtime::new() {\n            Ok(rt) => {\n                info!(\n                    \"⏱️ Created tokio runtime for chat backend in {:?}\",\n                    runtime_start.elapsed()\n                );\n                let backend_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n                match rt.block_on(ChatBackend::new(chat_config)) {\n                    Ok(backend) => {\n                        info!(\n                            \"⏱️ ✅ Chat backend initialized successfully in {:?} (total: {:?})\",\n                            backend_start.elapsed(),\n                            start.elapsed()\n                        );\n                        Some(backend)\n                    }\n                    Err(e) => {\n                        error!(\n                            \"⏱️ ❌ Failed to initialize chat backend: {} (took {:?})\",\n                            e,\n                            start.elapsed()\n                        );\n                        None\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                error!(\n                    \"⏱️ ❌ Failed to create tokio runtime: {} (took {:?})\",\n                    e,\n                    start.elapsed()\n                );\n                None\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Initialize chat backend from config.yaml with worker spawner support.\n    ///\n    /// Returns None if config is missing or initialization fails.\n    /// Errors are logged but don't prevent app startup.\n    fn init_chat_backend_with_worker_spawner(\n        _worker_runtime: &tokio::runtime::Runtime,\n        _worker_launcher: &WorkerLauncher,\n    ) -> Option<ChatBackend> {\n        use forge_chat::config::{AuditConfig, ConfirmationConfig, RateLimitConfig};\n        use forge_chat::spawner::RealWorkerSpawner;\n        use forge_chat::ChatProviderFactory;\n        use tracing::{error, info, warn};\n\n        let start = std::time::Instant::now();\n        info!(\"⏱️ init_chat_backend_with_worker_spawner() started\");\n\n        // Load config from ~/.forge/config.yaml\n        let config_path = match dirs::home_dir() {\n            Some(home) => home.join(\".forge/config.yaml\"),\n            None => {\n                warn!(\"⏱️ Could not determine home directory (took {:?})\", start.elapsed());\n                return None;\n            }\n        };\n\n        info!(\n            \"⏱️ Initializing chat backend from {}\",\n            config_path.display()\n        );\n\n        if !config_path.exists() {\n            warn!(\n                \"⏱️ Chat config not found at {} (took {:?})\",\n                config_path.display(),\n                start.elapsed()\n            );\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        let read_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n        let config_str = match std::fs::read_to_string(&config_path) {\n            Ok(s) => s,\n            Err(e) => {\n                error!(\n                    \"⏱️ Failed to read chat config: {} (took {:?})\",\n                    e,\n                    start.elapsed()\n                );\n                return None;\n            }\n        };\n        info!(\"⏱️ Config file read in {:?}\", read_start.elapsed());\n\n        // Parse the full config YAML\n        let parse_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n        let yaml: serde_yaml::Value = match serde_yaml::from_str(&config_str) {\n            Ok(v) => v,\n            Err(e) => {\n                warn!(\n                    \"⏱️ Failed to parse config YAML: {} (took {:?})\",\n                    e,\n                    start.elapsed()\n                );\n                return None;\n            }\n        };\n        info!(\"⏱️ YAML parsed in {:?}\", parse_start.elapsed());\n\n        // Extract chat_backend section\n        let chat_backend = yaml.get(\"chat_backend\")?;\n        let command = chat_backend.get(\"command\")?.as_str()?;\n\n        // Read optional args (not required — providers may handle args internally)\n        let args: Vec<String> = chat_backend\n            .get(\"args\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_sequence())\n            .map(|seq| {\n                seq.iter()\n                    .filter_map(|v| v.as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n                    .collect()\n            })\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n\n        // Read model (optional, defaults to \"sonnet\")\n        let model = chat_backend\n            .get(\"model\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .unwrap_or(\"sonnet\")\n            .to_string();\n\n        // Determine provider: explicit `provider` field takes precedence; fall back to\n        // inferring from `command` for backwards compatibility (opencode → opencode,\n        // anything else → claude-cli).\n        let provider_name = chat_backend\n            .get(\"provider\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| {\n                if command == \"opencode\" {\n                    \"opencode\"\n                } else {\n                    \"claude-cli\"\n                }\n            });\n\n        info!(\"⏱️ Using provider: {}\", provider_name);\n\n        // Warn about command/provider field mismatches before delegating to factory.\n        match provider_name {\n            \"opencode\" if command != \"opencode\" => {\n                warn!(\n                    \"Provider is 'opencode' but command is '{}' — using command as binary path\",\n                    command\n                );\n            }\n            \"claude-cli\" if command == \"opencode\" => {\n                warn!(\n                    \"Provider is 'claude-cli' but command is 'opencode' — this may not work as \\\n                     expected. Set provider: opencode to use the OpenCode provider.\"\n                );\n            }\n            _ => {}\n        }\n\n        // Read model_aliases (only consumed by the opencode provider).\n        let model_aliases = chat_backend\n            .get(\"model_aliases\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_mapping())\n            .map(|m| {\n                m.iter()\n                    .filter_map(|(k, v)| {\n                        let key = k.as_str()?.to_string();\n                        let val = v.as_str()?.to_string();\n                        Some((key, val))\n                    })\n                    .collect::<std::collections::HashMap<String, String>>()\n            });\n\n        // Use the factory to build the ProviderConfig from the provider name string.\n        let provider_config = match ChatProviderFactory::create_config(\n            provider_name,\n            command,\n            &model,\n            args,\n            model_aliases,\n        ) {\n            Ok(config) => config,\n            Err(e) => {\n                error!(\"Failed to create provider config: {}\", e);\n                return None;\n            }\n        };\n\n        let chat_config = ChatConfig {\n            provider: provider_config,\n            rate_limit: RateLimitConfig::default(),\n            audit: AuditConfig::default(),\n            confirmations: ConfirmationConfig::default(),\n        };\n\n        // Get workspace path\n        let home = std::env::var(\"HOME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"/home/coder\".to_string());\n        let workspace = std::env::var(\"FORGE_WORKSPACE\")\n            .map(std::path::PathBuf::from)\n            .unwrap_or_else(|_| std::path::PathBuf::from(&home).join(\"forge\"));\n\n        // Create RealWorkerSpawner with default launcher detection\n        let worker_spawner: Arc<dyn forge_chat::spawner::WorkerSpawner> = match RealWorkerSpawner::with_default_launcher(workspace) {\n            Some(spawner) => Arc::new(spawner),\n            None => {\n                warn!(\"⏱️ No worker launcher script found, worker spawning via chat will not be available\");\n                Arc::new(forge_chat::spawner::NoOpWorkerSpawner)\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Initialize backend (async, but we block here during startup)\n        let runtime_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n        match tokio::runtime::Runtime::new() {\n            Ok(rt) => {\n                info!(\n                    \"⏱️ Created tokio runtime for chat backend in {:?}\",\n                    runtime_start.elapsed()\n                );\n                let backend_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n                match rt.block_on(ChatBackend::new(chat_config)) {\n                    Ok(backend) => {\n                        let backend = backend.with_worker_spawner(worker_spawner);\n                        info!(\n                            \"⏱️ ✅ Chat backend initialized successfully in {:?} (total: {:?})\",\n                            backend_start.elapsed(),\n                            start.elapsed()\n                        );\n                        Some(backend)\n                    }\n                    Err(e) => {\n                        error!(\n                            \"⏱️ ❌ Failed to initialize chat backend: {} (took {:?})\",\n                            e,\n                            start.elapsed()\n                        );\n                        None\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                error!(\n                    \"⏱️ ❌ Failed to create tokio runtime: {} (took {:?})\",\n                    e,\n                    start.elapsed()\n                );\n                None\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the current view.\n    pub fn current_view(&self) -> View {\n        self.current_view\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the current focus panel.\n    pub fn focus_panel(&self) -> FocusPanel {\n        self.focus_panel\n    }\n\n    /// Returns whether the app should quit.\n    pub fn should_quit(&self) -> bool {\n        self.should_quit\n    }\n\n    /// Returns whether help overlay is visible.\n    pub fn show_help(&self) -> bool {\n        self.show_help\n    }\n\n    /// Returns whether kill dialog is visible.\n    pub fn show_kill_dialog(&self) -> bool {\n        self.show_kill_dialog\n    }\n\n    /// Mark the UI as dirty (needs redraw).\n    fn mark_dirty(&mut self) {\n        self.dirty = true;\n    }\n\n    /// Handle local chat commands (commands processed by TUI, not sent to backend).\n    ///\n    /// Returns true if the command was handled locally, false if it should be sent to backend.\n    fn handle_chat_command(&mut self, query: &str) -> bool {\n        let trimmed = query.trim();\n\n        // Check for slash commands\n        if !trimmed.starts_with('/') {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        // Parse command and arguments\n        let parts: Vec<&str> = trimmed[1..].splitn(2, ' ').collect();\n        let command = parts[0].to_lowercase();\n        let args = parts.get(1).map(|s| s.trim());\n\n        match command.as_str() {\n            \"save\" => {\n                self.save_chat_history();\n                true\n            }\n            \"load\" => {\n                self.load_chat_history();\n                true\n            }\n            \"export\" => {\n                let path = args.map(std::path::PathBuf::from);\n                self.export_chat_history(path);\n                true\n            }\n            \"clear\" => {\n                self.chat_history.clear();\n                self.chat_scroll_offset = 0;\n                self.status_message = Some(\"Chat history cleared\".to_string());\n                info!(\"Chat history cleared by user command\");\n                true\n            }\n            \"history\" => {\n                // Show history info\n                let count = self.chat_history.len();\n                let msg = if count == 0 {\n                    \"No chat history. Use /load to restore previous sessions.\".to_string()\n                } else {\n                    format!(\"Chat history: {} exchanges in current session\", count)\n                };\n                self.status_message = Some(msg);\n                true\n            }\n            \"stats\" => {\n                self.show_history_stats();\n                true\n            }\n            \"help\" => {\n                // Show available commands\n                let help_text = \"Commands: /save /load /export [path] /clear /history /stats /help\";\n                self.chat_history.push(ChatExchange {\n                    user_query: \"/help\".to_string(),\n                    assistant_response: help_text.to_string(),\n                    timestamp: chrono::Local::now().format(\"%H:%M:%S\").to_string(),\n                    is_error: false,\n                    tool_calls: vec![],\n                    side_effects: vec![],\n                    confirmation: None,\n                    metadata: ResponseMetadata::default(),\n                    error_guidance: None,\n                });\n                // Limit history to 10 exchanges\n                if self.chat_history.len() > 10 {\n                    self.chat_history.remove(0);\n                }\n                true\n            }\n            _ => {\n                // Unknown slash command - let it go to the backend\n                false\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Save current chat history to disk.\n    fn save_chat_history(&mut self) {\n        let history_manager = match &self.history_manager {\n            Some(hm) => hm,\n            None => {\n                self.status_message = Some(\"History manager not initialized\".to_string());\n                return;\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Convert ChatExchange to HistoryEntry\n        let entries: Vec<forge_chat::HistoryEntry> = self\n            .chat_history\n            .iter()\n            .map(|exchange| forge_chat::HistoryEntry {\n                timestamp: exchange.timestamp.clone(),\n                user_query: exchange.user_query.clone(),\n                assistant_response: exchange.assistant_response.clone(),\n                is_error: exchange.is_error,\n                metadata: Some(forge_chat::HistoryMetadata {\n                    duration_ms: exchange.metadata.duration_ms,\n                    cost_usd: exchange.metadata.cost_usd,\n                    provider: exchange.metadata.provider.clone(),\n                }),\n                tool_calls: exchange\n                    .tool_calls\n                    .iter()\n                    .map(|tc| forge_chat::HistoryToolCall {\n                        name: tc.name.clone(),\n                        success: tc.success,\n                        message: Some(tc.message.clone()),\n                    })\n                    .collect(),\n                session_id: None,\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        // Save in background (avoid blocking UI)\n        let history_path = history_manager.history_path().clone();\n        let session_id = history_manager.session_id().to_string();\n        std::thread::spawn(move || {\n            use std::io::Write;\n            match std::fs::OpenOptions::new()\n                .create(true)\n                .append(true)\n                .open(&history_path)\n            {\n                Ok(mut file) => {\n                    let mut saved = 0;\n                    for mut entry in entries {\n                        entry.session_id = Some(session_id.clone());\n                        if let Ok(json) = serde_json::to_string(&entry) {\n                            if writeln!(file, \"{}\", json).is_ok() {\n                                saved += 1;\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                    info!(\"Saved {} entries to
